src/backend/parser/analyze.c
1/*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
2 *
3 * analyze.c
4 *	  transform the raw parse tree into a query tree
5 *
6 * For optimizable statements, we are careful to obtain a suitable lock on
7 * each referenced table, and other modules of the backend preserve or
8 * re-obtain these locks before depending on the results.  It is therefore
9 * okay to do significant semantic analysis of these statements.  For
10 * utility commands, no locks are obtained here (and if they were, we could
11 * not be sure we'd still have them at execution).  Hence the general rule
12 * for utility commands is to just dump them into a Query node untransformed.
13 * DECLARE CURSOR, EXPLAIN, and CREATE TABLE AS are exceptions because they
14 * contain optimizable statements, which we should transform.
15 *
16 *
17 * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
18 * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
19 *
20 *	src/backend/parser/analyze.c
21 *
22 *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
23 */
24
25#include "postgres.h"
26
27#include "access/sysattr.h"
28#include "catalog/pg_proc.h"
29#include "catalog/pg_type.h"

32#include "nodes/makefuncs.h"
33#include "nodes/nodeFuncs.h"
34#include "nodes/queryjumble.h"
35#include "optimizer/optimizer.h"
36#include "parser/analyze.h"
37#include "parser/parse_agg.h"
38#include "parser/parse_clause.h"
39#include "parser/parse_coerce.h"
40#include "parser/parse_collate.h"
41#include "parser/parse_cte.h"
42#include "parser/parse_expr.h"
43#include "parser/parse_func.h"
44#include "parser/parse_merge.h"
45#include "parser/parse_oper.h"
46#include "parser/parse_param.h"
47#include "parser/parse_relation.h"
48#include "parser/parse_target.h"
49#include "parser/parse_type.h"
50#include "parser/parsetree.h"
51#include "utils/backend_status.h"
52#include "utils/builtins.h"
53#include "utils/guc.h"
54#include "utils/rel.h"
55#include "utils/syscache.h"
56

58/* Hook for plugins to get control at end of parse analysis */
59post_parse_analyze_hook_type post_parse_analyze_hook = NULL;
60
61static Query *transformOptionalSelectInto(ParseState *pstate, Node *parseTree);
62static Query *transformDeleteStmt(ParseState *pstate, DeleteStmt *stmt);
63static Query *transformInsertStmt(ParseState *pstate, InsertStmt *stmt);
64static OnConflictExpr *transformOnConflictClause(ParseState *pstate,
65												 OnConflictClause *onConflictClause);
66static int	count_rowexpr_columns(ParseState *pstate, Node *expr);
67static Query *transformSelectStmt(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt);
68static Query *transformValuesClause(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt);
69static Query *transformSetOperationStmt(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt);
70static Node *transformSetOperationTree(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt,
71									   bool isTopLevel, List **targetlist);
72static void determineRecursiveColTypes(ParseState *pstate,
73									   Node *larg, List *nrtargetlist);
74static Query *transformReturnStmt(ParseState *pstate, ReturnStmt *stmt);
75static Query *transformUpdateStmt(ParseState *pstate, UpdateStmt *stmt);
76static Query *transformPLAssignStmt(ParseState *pstate,
77									PLAssignStmt *stmt);
78static Query *transformDeclareCursorStmt(ParseState *pstate,
79										 DeclareCursorStmt *stmt);
80static Query *transformExplainStmt(ParseState *pstate,
81								   ExplainStmt *stmt);
82static Query *transformCreateTableAsStmt(ParseState *pstate,
83										 CreateTableAsStmt *stmt);
84static Query *transformCallStmt(ParseState *pstate,
85								CallStmt *stmt);
86static void transformLockingClause(ParseState *pstate, Query *qry,
87								   LockingClause *lc, bool pushedDown);
88#ifdef DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED
89static bool test_raw_expression_coverage(Node *node, void *context);
90#endif
91
92
93/*
94 * parse_analyze_fixedparams
95 *		Analyze a raw parse tree and transform it to Query form.
96 *
97 * Optionally, information about $n parameter types can be supplied.
98 * References to $n indexes not defined by paramTypes[] are disallowed.
99 *
100 * The result is a Query node.  Optimizable statements require considerable
101 * transformation, while utility-type statements are simply hung off
102 * a dummy CMD_UTILITY Query node.
103 */
104Query *
105parse_analyze_fixedparams(RawStmt *parseTree, const char *sourceText,
106						  const Oid *paramTypes, int numParams,
107						  QueryEnvironment *queryEnv)
108{
109	ParseState *pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
110	Query	   *query;
111	JumbleState *jstate = NULL;
112
113	Assert(sourceText != NULL); /* required as of 8.4 */
114
115	pstate->p_sourcetext = sourceText;
116
117	if (numParams > 0)
118		setup_parse_fixed_parameters(pstate, paramTypes, numParams);
119
120	pstate->p_queryEnv = queryEnv;
121
122	query = transformTopLevelStmt(pstate, parseTree);
123
124	if (IsQueryIdEnabled())
125		jstate = JumbleQuery(query);
126
127	if (post_parse_analyze_hook)
128		(*post_parse_analyze_hook) (pstate, query, jstate);
129
130	free_parsestate(pstate);
131
132	pgstat_report_query_id(query->queryId, false);
133
134	return query;
135}
136
137/*
138 * parse_analyze_varparams
139 *
140 * This variant is used when it's okay to deduce information about $n
141 * symbol datatypes from context.  The passed-in paramTypes[] array can
142 * be modified or enlarged (via repalloc).
143 */
144Query *
145parse_analyze_varparams(RawStmt *parseTree, const char *sourceText,
146						Oid **paramTypes, int *numParams,
147						QueryEnvironment *queryEnv)
148{
149	ParseState *pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
150	Query	   *query;
151	JumbleState *jstate = NULL;
152
153	Assert(sourceText != NULL); /* required as of 8.4 */
154
155	pstate->p_sourcetext = sourceText;
156
157	setup_parse_variable_parameters(pstate, paramTypes, numParams);
158
159	pstate->p_queryEnv = queryEnv;
160
161	query = transformTopLevelStmt(pstate, parseTree);
162
163	/* make sure all is well with parameter types */
164	check_variable_parameters(pstate, query);
165
166	if (IsQueryIdEnabled())
167		jstate = JumbleQuery(query);
168
169	if (post_parse_analyze_hook)
170		(*post_parse_analyze_hook) (pstate, query, jstate);
171
172	free_parsestate(pstate);
173
174	pgstat_report_query_id(query->queryId, false);
175
176	return query;
177}
178
179/*
180 * parse_analyze_withcb
181 *
182 * This variant is used when the caller supplies their own parser callback to
183 * resolve parameters and possibly other things.
184 */
185Query *
186parse_analyze_withcb(RawStmt *parseTree, const char *sourceText,
187					 ParserSetupHook parserSetup,
188					 void *parserSetupArg,
189					 QueryEnvironment *queryEnv)
190{
191	ParseState *pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
192	Query	   *query;
193	JumbleState *jstate = NULL;
194
195	Assert(sourceText != NULL); /* required as of 8.4 */
196
197	pstate->p_sourcetext = sourceText;
198	pstate->p_queryEnv = queryEnv;
199	(*parserSetup) (pstate, parserSetupArg);
200
201	query = transformTopLevelStmt(pstate, parseTree);
202
203	if (IsQueryIdEnabled())
204		jstate = JumbleQuery(query);
205
206	if (post_parse_analyze_hook)
207		(*post_parse_analyze_hook) (pstate, query, jstate);
208
209	free_parsestate(pstate);
210
211	pgstat_report_query_id(query->queryId, false);
212
213	return query;
214}
215
216
217/*
218 * parse_sub_analyze
219 *		Entry point for recursively analyzing a sub-statement.
220 */
221Query *
222parse_sub_analyze(Node *parseTree, ParseState *parentParseState,
223				  CommonTableExpr *parentCTE,
224				  bool locked_from_parent,
225				  bool resolve_unknowns)
226{
227	ParseState *pstate = make_parsestate(parentParseState);
228	Query	   *query;
229
230	pstate->p_parent_cte = parentCTE;
231	pstate->p_locked_from_parent = locked_from_parent;
232	pstate->p_resolve_unknowns = resolve_unknowns;
233
234	query = transformStmt(pstate, parseTree);
235
236	free_parsestate(pstate);
237
238	return query;
239}
240
241/*
242 * transformTopLevelStmt -
243 *	  transform a Parse tree into a Query tree.
244 *
245 * This function is just responsible for transferring statement location data
246 * from the RawStmt into the finished Query.
247 */
248Query *
249transformTopLevelStmt(ParseState *pstate, RawStmt *parseTree)
250{
251	Query	   *result;
252
253	/* We're at top level, so allow SELECT INTO */
254	result = transformOptionalSelectInto(pstate, parseTree->stmt);
255
256	result->stmt_location = parseTree->stmt_location;
257	result->stmt_len = parseTree->stmt_len;
258
259	return result;
260}
261
262/*
263 * transformOptionalSelectInto -
264 *	  If SELECT has INTO, convert it to CREATE TABLE AS.
265 *
266 * The only thing we do here that we don't do in transformStmt() is to
267 * convert SELECT ... INTO into CREATE TABLE AS.  Since utility statements
268 * aren't allowed within larger statements, this is only allowed at the top
269 * of the parse tree, and so we only try it before entering the recursive
270 * transformStmt() processing.
271 */
272static Query *
273transformOptionalSelectInto(ParseState *pstate, Node *parseTree)
274{
275	if (IsA(parseTree, SelectStmt))
276	{
277		SelectStmt *stmt = (SelectStmt *) parseTree;
278
279		/* If it's a set-operation tree, drill down to leftmost SelectStmt */
280		while (stmt && stmt->op != SETOP_NONE)
281			stmt = stmt->larg;
282		Assert(stmt && IsA(stmt, SelectStmt) && stmt->larg == NULL);
283
284		if (stmt->intoClause)
285		{
286			CreateTableAsStmt *ctas = makeNode(CreateTableAsStmt);
287
288			ctas->query = parseTree;
289			ctas->into = stmt->intoClause;
290			ctas->objtype = OBJECT_TABLE;
291			ctas->is_select_into = true;
292
293			/*
294			 * Remove the intoClause from the SelectStmt.  This makes it safe
295			 * for transformSelectStmt to complain if it finds intoClause set
296			 * (implying that the INTO appeared in a disallowed place).
297			 */
298			stmt->intoClause = NULL;
299
300			parseTree = (Node *) ctas;
301		}
302	}
303
304	return transformStmt(pstate, parseTree);
305}
306
307/*
308 * transformStmt -
309 *	  recursively transform a Parse tree into a Query tree.
310 */
311Query *
312transformStmt(ParseState *pstate, Node *parseTree)
313{
314	Query	   *result;
315
316#ifdef DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED
317
318	/*
319	 * We apply debug_raw_expression_coverage_test testing to basic DML
320	 * statements; we can't just run it on everything because
321	 * raw_expression_tree_walker() doesn't claim to handle utility
322	 * statements.
323	 */
324	if (Debug_raw_expression_coverage_test)
325	{
326		switch (nodeTag(parseTree))
327		{
328			case T_SelectStmt:
329			case T_InsertStmt:
330			case T_UpdateStmt:
331			case T_DeleteStmt:
332			case T_MergeStmt:
333				(void) test_raw_expression_coverage(parseTree, NULL);
334				break;
335			default:
336				break;
337		}
338	}
339#endif							/* DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED */
340
341	/*
342	 * Caution: when changing the set of statement types that have non-default
343	 * processing here, see also stmt_requires_parse_analysis() and
344	 * analyze_requires_snapshot().
345	 */
346	switch (nodeTag(parseTree))
347	{
348			/*
349			 * Optimizable statements
350			 */
351		case T_InsertStmt:
352			result = transformInsertStmt(pstate, (InsertStmt *) parseTree);
353			break;
354
355		case T_DeleteStmt:
356			result = transformDeleteStmt(pstate, (DeleteStmt *) parseTree);
357			break;
358
359		case T_UpdateStmt:
360			result = transformUpdateStmt(pstate, (UpdateStmt *) parseTree);
361			break;
362
363		case T_MergeStmt:
364			result = transformMergeStmt(pstate, (MergeStmt *) parseTree);
365			break;
366
367		case T_SelectStmt:
368			{
369				SelectStmt *n = (SelectStmt *) parseTree;
370
371				if (n->valuesLists)
372					result = transformValuesClause(pstate, n);
373				else if (n->op == SETOP_NONE)
374					result = transformSelectStmt(pstate, n);
375				else
376					result = transformSetOperationStmt(pstate, n);
377			}
378			break;
379
380		case T_ReturnStmt:
381			result = transformReturnStmt(pstate, (ReturnStmt *) parseTree);
382			break;
383
384		case T_PLAssignStmt:
385			result = transformPLAssignStmt(pstate,
386										   (PLAssignStmt *) parseTree);
387			break;
388
389			/*
390			 * Special cases
391			 */
392		case T_DeclareCursorStmt:
393			result = transformDeclareCursorStmt(pstate,
394												(DeclareCursorStmt *) parseTree);
395			break;
396
397		case T_ExplainStmt:
398			result = transformExplainStmt(pstate,
399										  (ExplainStmt *) parseTree);
400			break;
401
402		case T_CreateTableAsStmt:
403			result = transformCreateTableAsStmt(pstate,
404												(CreateTableAsStmt *) parseTree);
405			break;
406
407		case T_CallStmt:
408			result = transformCallStmt(pstate,
409									   (CallStmt *) parseTree);
410			break;
411
412		default:
413
414			/*
415			 * other statements don't require any transformation; just return
416			 * the original parsetree with a Query node plastered on top.
417			 */
418			result = makeNode(Query);
419			result->commandType = CMD_UTILITY;
420			result->utilityStmt = (Node *) parseTree;
421			break;
422	}
423
424	/* Mark as original query until we learn differently */
425	result->querySource = QSRC_ORIGINAL;
426	result->canSetTag = true;
427
428	return result;
429}
430
431/*
432 * stmt_requires_parse_analysis
433 *		Returns true if parse analysis will do anything non-trivial
434 *		with the given raw parse tree.
435 *
436 * Generally, this should return true for any statement type for which
437 * transformStmt() does more than wrap a CMD_UTILITY Query around it.
438 * When it returns false, the caller can assume that there is no situation
439 * in which parse analysis of the raw statement could need to be re-done.
440 *
441 * Currently, since the rewriter and planner do nothing for CMD_UTILITY
442 * Queries, a false result means that the entire parse analysis/rewrite/plan
443 * pipeline will never need to be re-done.  If that ever changes, callers
444 * will likely need adjustment.
445 */
446bool
447stmt_requires_parse_analysis(RawStmt *parseTree)
448{
449	bool		result;
450
451	switch (nodeTag(parseTree->stmt))
452	{
453			/*
454			 * Optimizable statements
455			 */
456		case T_InsertStmt:
457		case T_DeleteStmt:
458		case T_UpdateStmt:
459		case T_MergeStmt:
460		case T_SelectStmt:
461		case T_ReturnStmt:
462		case T_PLAssignStmt:
463			result = true;
464			break;
465
466			/*
467			 * Special cases
468			 */
469		case T_DeclareCursorStmt:
470		case T_ExplainStmt:
471		case T_CreateTableAsStmt:
472		case T_CallStmt:
473			result = true;
474			break;
475
476		default:
477			/* all other statements just get wrapped in a CMD_UTILITY Query */
478			result = false;
479			break;
480	}
481
482	return result;
483}
484
485/*
486 * analyze_requires_snapshot
487 *		Returns true if a snapshot must be set before doing parse analysis
488 *		on the given raw parse tree.
489 */
490bool
491analyze_requires_snapshot(RawStmt *parseTree)
492{
493	/*
494	 * Currently, this should return true in exactly the same cases that
495	 * stmt_requires_parse_analysis() does, so we just invoke that function
496	 * rather than duplicating it.  We keep the two entry points separate for
497	 * clarity of callers, since from the callers' standpoint these are
498	 * different conditions.
499	 *
500	 * While there may someday be a statement type for which transformStmt()
501	 * does something nontrivial and yet no snapshot is needed for that
502	 * processing, it seems likely that making such a choice would be fragile.
503	 * If you want to install an exception, document the reasoning for it in a
504	 * comment.
505	 */
506	return stmt_requires_parse_analysis(parseTree);
507}
508
509/*
510 * query_requires_rewrite_plan()
511 *		Returns true if rewriting or planning is non-trivial for this Query.
512 *
513 * This is much like stmt_requires_parse_analysis(), but applies one step
514 * further down the pipeline.
515 *
516 * We do not provide an equivalent of analyze_requires_snapshot(): callers
517 * can assume that any rewriting or planning activity needs a snapshot.
518 */
519bool
520query_requires_rewrite_plan(Query *query)
521{
522	bool		result;
523
524	if (query->commandType != CMD_UTILITY)
525	{
526		/* All optimizable statements require rewriting/planning */
527		result = true;
528	}
529	else
530	{
531		/* This list should match stmt_requires_parse_analysis() */
532		switch (nodeTag(query->utilityStmt))
533		{
534			case T_DeclareCursorStmt:
535			case T_ExplainStmt:
536			case T_CreateTableAsStmt:
537			case T_CallStmt:
538				result = true;
539				break;
540			default:
541				result = false;
542				break;
543		}
544	}
545	return result;
546}
547
548/*
549 * transformDeleteStmt -
550 *	  transforms a Delete Statement
551 */
552static Query *
553transformDeleteStmt(ParseState *pstate, DeleteStmt *stmt)
554{
555	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
556	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
557	Node	   *qual;
558
559	qry->commandType = CMD_DELETE;
560
561	/* process the WITH clause independently of all else */
562	if (stmt->withClause)
563	{
564		qry->hasRecursive = stmt->withClause->recursive;
565		qry->cteList = transformWithClause(pstate, stmt->withClause);
566		qry->hasModifyingCTE = pstate->p_hasModifyingCTE;
567	}
568
569	/* set up range table with just the result rel */
570	qry->resultRelation = setTargetTable(pstate, stmt->relation,
571										 stmt->relation->inh,
572										 true,
573										 ACL_DELETE);
574	nsitem = pstate->p_target_nsitem;
575
576	/* disallow DELETE ... WHERE CURRENT OF on a view */
577	if (stmt->whereClause &&
578		IsA(stmt->whereClause, CurrentOfExpr) &&
579		pstate->p_target_relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW)
580		ereport(ERROR,
581				errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
582				errmsg("WHERE CURRENT OF on a view is not implemented"));
583
584	/* there's no DISTINCT in DELETE */
585	qry->distinctClause = NIL;
586
587	/* subqueries in USING cannot access the result relation */
588	nsitem->p_lateral_only = true;
589	nsitem->p_lateral_ok = false;
590
591	/*
592	 * The USING clause is non-standard SQL syntax, and is equivalent in
593	 * functionality to the FROM list that can be specified for UPDATE. The
594	 * USING keyword is used rather than FROM because FROM is already a
595	 * keyword in the DELETE syntax.
596	 */
597	transformFromClause(pstate, stmt->usingClause);
598
599	/* remaining clauses can reference the result relation normally */
600	nsitem->p_lateral_only = false;
601	nsitem->p_lateral_ok = true;
602
603	qual = transformWhereClause(pstate, stmt->whereClause,
604								EXPR_KIND_WHERE, "WHERE");
605
606	transformReturningClause(pstate, qry, stmt->returningClause,
607							 EXPR_KIND_RETURNING);
608
609	/* done building the range table and jointree */
610	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
611	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
612	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, qual);
613
614	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
615	qry->hasWindowFuncs = pstate->p_hasWindowFuncs;
616	qry->hasTargetSRFs = pstate->p_hasTargetSRFs;
617	qry->hasAggs = pstate->p_hasAggs;
618
619	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
620
621	/* this must be done after collations, for reliable comparison of exprs */
622	if (pstate->p_hasAggs)
623		parseCheckAggregates(pstate, qry);
624
625	return qry;
626}
627
628/*
629 * transformInsertStmt -
630 *	  transform an Insert Statement
631 */
632static Query *
633transformInsertStmt(ParseState *pstate, InsertStmt *stmt)
634{
635	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
636	SelectStmt *selectStmt = (SelectStmt *) stmt->selectStmt;
637	List	   *exprList = NIL;
638	bool		isGeneralSelect;
639	List	   *sub_rtable;
640	List	   *sub_rteperminfos;
641	List	   *sub_namespace;
642	List	   *icolumns;
643	List	   *attrnos;
644	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
645	RTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;
646	ListCell   *icols;
647	ListCell   *attnos;
648	ListCell   *lc;
649	bool		isOnConflictUpdate;
650	AclMode		targetPerms;
651
652	/* There can't be any outer WITH to worry about */
653	Assert(pstate->p_ctenamespace == NIL);
654
655	qry->commandType = CMD_INSERT;
656	pstate->p_is_insert = true;
657
658	/* process the WITH clause independently of all else */
659	if (stmt->withClause)
660	{
661		qry->hasRecursive = stmt->withClause->recursive;
662		qry->cteList = transformWithClause(pstate, stmt->withClause);
663		qry->hasModifyingCTE = pstate->p_hasModifyingCTE;
664	}
665
666	qry->override = stmt->override;
667
668	isOnConflictUpdate = (stmt->onConflictClause &&
669						  stmt->onConflictClause->action == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE);
670
671	/*
672	 * We have three cases to deal with: DEFAULT VALUES (selectStmt == NULL),
673	 * VALUES list, or general SELECT input.  We special-case VALUES, both for
674	 * efficiency and so we can handle DEFAULT specifications.
675	 *
676	 * The grammar allows attaching ORDER BY, LIMIT, FOR UPDATE, or WITH to a
677	 * VALUES clause.  If we have any of those, treat it as a general SELECT;
678	 * so it will work, but you can't use DEFAULT items together with those.
679	 */
680	isGeneralSelect = (selectStmt && (selectStmt->valuesLists == NIL ||
681									  selectStmt->sortClause != NIL ||
682									  selectStmt->limitOffset != NULL ||
683									  selectStmt->limitCount != NULL ||
684									  selectStmt->lockingClause != NIL ||
685									  selectStmt->withClause != NULL));
686
687	/*
688	 * If a non-nil rangetable/namespace was passed in, and we are doing
689	 * INSERT/SELECT, arrange to pass the rangetable/rteperminfos/namespace
690	 * down to the SELECT.  This can only happen if we are inside a CREATE
691	 * RULE, and in that case we want the rule's OLD and NEW rtable entries to
692	 * appear as part of the SELECT's rtable, not as outer references for it.
693	 * (Kluge!) The SELECT's joinlist is not affected however.  We must do
694	 * this before adding the target table to the INSERT's rtable.
695	 */
696	if (isGeneralSelect)
697	{
698		sub_rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
699		pstate->p_rtable = NIL;
700		sub_rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
701		pstate->p_rteperminfos = NIL;
702		sub_namespace = pstate->p_namespace;
703		pstate->p_namespace = NIL;
704	}
705	else
706	{
707		sub_rtable = NIL;		/* not used, but keep compiler quiet */
708		sub_rteperminfos = NIL;
709		sub_namespace = NIL;
710	}
711
712	/*
713	 * Must get write lock on INSERT target table before scanning SELECT, else
714	 * we will grab the wrong kind of initial lock if the target table is also
715	 * mentioned in the SELECT part.  Note that the target table is not added
716	 * to the joinlist or namespace.
717	 */
718	targetPerms = ACL_INSERT;
719	if (isOnConflictUpdate)
720		targetPerms |= ACL_UPDATE;
721	qry->resultRelation = setTargetTable(pstate, stmt->relation,
722										 false, false, targetPerms);
723
724	/* Validate stmt->cols list, or build default list if no list given */
725	icolumns = checkInsertTargets(pstate, stmt->cols, &attrnos);
726	Assert(list_length(icolumns) == list_length(attrnos));
727
728	/*
729	 * Determine which variant of INSERT we have.
730	 */
731	if (selectStmt == NULL)
732	{
733		/*
734		 * We have INSERT ... DEFAULT VALUES.  We can handle this case by
735		 * emitting an empty targetlist --- all columns will be defaulted when
736		 * the planner expands the targetlist.
737		 */
738		exprList = NIL;
739	}
740	else if (isGeneralSelect)
741	{
742		/*
743		 * We make the sub-pstate a child of the outer pstate so that it can
744		 * see any Param definitions supplied from above.  Since the outer
745		 * pstate's rtable and namespace are presently empty, there are no
746		 * side-effects of exposing names the sub-SELECT shouldn't be able to
747		 * see.
748		 */
749		ParseState *sub_pstate = make_parsestate(pstate);
750		Query	   *selectQuery;
751
752		/*
753		 * Process the source SELECT.
754		 *
755		 * It is important that this be handled just like a standalone SELECT;
756		 * otherwise the behavior of SELECT within INSERT might be different
757		 * from a stand-alone SELECT. (Indeed, Postgres up through 6.5 had
758		 * bugs of just that nature...)
759		 *
760		 * The sole exception is that we prevent resolving unknown-type
761		 * outputs as TEXT.  This does not change the semantics since if the
762		 * column type matters semantically, it would have been resolved to
763		 * something else anyway.  Doing this lets us resolve such outputs as
764		 * the target column's type, which we handle below.
765		 */
766		sub_pstate->p_rtable = sub_rtable;
767		sub_pstate->p_rteperminfos = sub_rteperminfos;
768		sub_pstate->p_joinexprs = NIL;	/* sub_rtable has no joins */
769		sub_pstate->p_nullingrels = NIL;
770		sub_pstate->p_namespace = sub_namespace;
771		sub_pstate->p_resolve_unknowns = false;
772
773		selectQuery = transformStmt(sub_pstate, stmt->selectStmt);
774
775		free_parsestate(sub_pstate);
776
777		/* The grammar should have produced a SELECT */
778		if (!IsA(selectQuery, Query) ||
779			selectQuery->commandType != CMD_SELECT)
780			elog(ERROR, "unexpected non-SELECT command in INSERT ... SELECT");
781
782		/*
783		 * Make the source be a subquery in the INSERT's rangetable, and add
784		 * it to the INSERT's joinlist (but not the namespace).
785		 */
786		nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForSubquery(pstate,
787											   selectQuery,
788											   makeAlias("*SELECT*", NIL),
789											   false,
790											   false);
791		addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, true, false, false);
792
793		/*----------
794		 * Generate an expression list for the INSERT that selects all the
795		 * non-resjunk columns from the subquery.  (INSERT's tlist must be
796		 * separate from the subquery's tlist because we may add columns,
797		 * insert datatype coercions, etc.)
798		 *
799		 * HACK: unknown-type constants and params in the SELECT's targetlist
800		 * are copied up as-is rather than being referenced as subquery
801		 * outputs.  This is to ensure that when we try to coerce them to
802		 * the target column's datatype, the right things happen (see
803		 * special cases in coerce_type).  Otherwise, this fails:
804		 *		INSERT INTO foo SELECT 'bar', ... FROM baz
805		 *----------
806		 */
807		exprList = NIL;
808		foreach(lc, selectQuery->targetList)
809		{
810			TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(lc);
811			Expr	   *expr;
812
813			if (tle->resjunk)
814				continue;
815			if (tle->expr &&
816				(IsA(tle->expr, Const) || IsA(tle->expr, Param)) &&
817				exprType((Node *) tle->expr) == UNKNOWNOID)
818				expr = tle->expr;
819			else
820			{
821				Var		   *var = makeVarFromTargetEntry(nsitem->p_rtindex, tle);
822
823				var->location = exprLocation((Node *) tle->expr);
824				expr = (Expr *) var;
825			}
826			exprList = lappend(exprList, expr);
827		}
828
829		/* Prepare row for assignment to target table */
830		exprList = transformInsertRow(pstate, exprList,
831									  stmt->cols,
832									  icolumns, attrnos,
833									  false);
834	}
835	else if (list_length(selectStmt->valuesLists) > 1)
836	{
837		/*
838		 * Process INSERT ... VALUES with multiple VALUES sublists. We
839		 * generate a VALUES RTE holding the transformed expression lists, and
840		 * build up a targetlist containing Vars that reference the VALUES
841		 * RTE.
842		 */
843		List	   *exprsLists = NIL;
844		List	   *coltypes = NIL;
845		List	   *coltypmods = NIL;
846		List	   *colcollations = NIL;
847		int			sublist_length = -1;
848		bool		lateral = false;
849
850		Assert(selectStmt->intoClause == NULL);
851
852		foreach(lc, selectStmt->valuesLists)
853		{
854			List	   *sublist = (List *) lfirst(lc);
855
856			/*
857			 * Do basic expression transformation (same as a ROW() expr, but
858			 * allow SetToDefault at top level)
859			 */
860			sublist = transformExpressionList(pstate, sublist,
861											  EXPR_KIND_VALUES, true);
862
863			/*
864			 * All the sublists must be the same length, *after*
865			 * transformation (which might expand '*' into multiple items).
866			 * The VALUES RTE can't handle anything different.
867			 */
868			if (sublist_length < 0)
869			{
870				/* Remember post-transformation length of first sublist */
871				sublist_length = list_length(sublist);
872			}
873			else if (sublist_length != list_length(sublist))
874			{
875				ereport(ERROR,
876						(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
877						 errmsg("VALUES lists must all be the same length"),
878						 parser_errposition(pstate,
879											exprLocation((Node *) sublist))));
880			}
881
882			/*
883			 * Prepare row for assignment to target table.  We process any
884			 * indirection on the target column specs normally but then strip
885			 * off the resulting field/array assignment nodes, since we don't
886			 * want the parsed statement to contain copies of those in each
887			 * VALUES row.  (It's annoying to have to transform the
888			 * indirection specs over and over like this, but avoiding it
889			 * would take some really messy refactoring of
890			 * transformAssignmentIndirection.)
891			 */
892			sublist = transformInsertRow(pstate, sublist,
893										 stmt->cols,
894										 icolumns, attrnos,
895										 true);
896
897			/*
898			 * We must assign collations now because assign_query_collations
899			 * doesn't process rangetable entries.  We just assign all the
900			 * collations independently in each row, and don't worry about
901			 * whether they are consistent vertically.  The outer INSERT query
902			 * isn't going to care about the collations of the VALUES columns,
903			 * so it's not worth the effort to identify a common collation for
904			 * each one here.  (But note this does have one user-visible
905			 * consequence: INSERT ... VALUES won't complain about conflicting
906			 * explicit COLLATEs in a column, whereas the same VALUES
907			 * construct in another context would complain.)
908			 */
909			assign_list_collations(pstate, sublist);
910
911			exprsLists = lappend(exprsLists, sublist);
912		}
913
914		/*
915		 * Construct column type/typmod/collation lists for the VALUES RTE.
916		 * Every expression in each column has been coerced to the type/typmod
917		 * of the corresponding target column or subfield, so it's sufficient
918		 * to look at the exprType/exprTypmod of the first row.  We don't care
919		 * about the collation labeling, so just fill in InvalidOid for that.
920		 */
921		foreach(lc, (List *) linitial(exprsLists))
922		{
923			Node	   *val = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
924
925			coltypes = lappend_oid(coltypes, exprType(val));
926			coltypmods = lappend_int(coltypmods, exprTypmod(val));
927			colcollations = lappend_oid(colcollations, InvalidOid);
928		}
929
930		/*
931		 * Ordinarily there can't be any current-level Vars in the expression
932		 * lists, because the namespace was empty ... but if we're inside
933		 * CREATE RULE, then NEW/OLD references might appear.  In that case we
934		 * have to mark the VALUES RTE as LATERAL.
935		 */
936		if (list_length(pstate->p_rtable) != 1 &&
937			contain_vars_of_level((Node *) exprsLists, 0))
938			lateral = true;
939
940		/*
941		 * Generate the VALUES RTE
942		 */
943		nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForValues(pstate, exprsLists,
944											 coltypes, coltypmods, colcollations,
945											 NULL, lateral, true);
946		addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, true, false, false);
947
948		/*
949		 * Generate list of Vars referencing the RTE
950		 */
951		exprList = expandNSItemVars(pstate, nsitem, 0, -1, NULL);
952
953		/*
954		 * Re-apply any indirection on the target column specs to the Vars
955		 */
956		exprList = transformInsertRow(pstate, exprList,
957									  stmt->cols,
958									  icolumns, attrnos,
959									  false);
960	}
961	else
962	{
963		/*
964		 * Process INSERT ... VALUES with a single VALUES sublist.  We treat
965		 * this case separately for efficiency.  The sublist is just computed
966		 * directly as the Query's targetlist, with no VALUES RTE.  So it
967		 * works just like a SELECT without any FROM.
968		 */
969		List	   *valuesLists = selectStmt->valuesLists;
970
971		Assert(list_length(valuesLists) == 1);
972		Assert(selectStmt->intoClause == NULL);
973
974		/*
975		 * Do basic expression transformation (same as a ROW() expr, but allow
976		 * SetToDefault at top level)
977		 */
978		exprList = transformExpressionList(pstate,
979										   (List *) linitial(valuesLists),
980										   EXPR_KIND_VALUES_SINGLE,
981										   true);
982
983		/* Prepare row for assignment to target table */
984		exprList = transformInsertRow(pstate, exprList,
985									  stmt->cols,
986									  icolumns, attrnos,
987									  false);
988	}
989
990	/*
991	 * Generate query's target list using the computed list of expressions.
992	 * Also, mark all the target columns as needing insert permissions.
993	 */
994	perminfo = pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_perminfo;
995	qry->targetList = NIL;
996	Assert(list_length(exprList) <= list_length(icolumns));
997	forthree(lc, exprList, icols, icolumns, attnos, attrnos)
998	{
999		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
1000		ResTarget  *col = lfirst_node(ResTarget, icols);
1001		AttrNumber	attr_num = (AttrNumber) lfirst_int(attnos);
1002		TargetEntry *tle;
1003
1004		tle = makeTargetEntry(expr,
1005							  attr_num,
1006							  col->name,
1007							  false);
1008		qry->targetList = lappend(qry->targetList, tle);
1009
1010		perminfo->insertedCols = bms_add_member(perminfo->insertedCols,
1011												attr_num - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
1012	}
1013
1014	/*
1015	 * If we have any clauses yet to process, set the query namespace to
1016	 * contain only the target relation, removing any entries added in a
1017	 * sub-SELECT or VALUES list.
1018	 */
1019	if (stmt->onConflictClause || stmt->returningClause)
1020	{
1021		pstate->p_namespace = NIL;
1022		addNSItemToQuery(pstate, pstate->p_target_nsitem,
1023						 false, true, true);
1024	}
1025
1026	/* Process ON CONFLICT, if any. */
1027	if (stmt->onConflictClause)
1028		qry->onConflict = transformOnConflictClause(pstate,
1029													stmt->onConflictClause);
1030
1031	/* Process RETURNING, if any. */
1032	if (stmt->returningClause)
1033		transformReturningClause(pstate, qry, stmt->returningClause,
1034								 EXPR_KIND_RETURNING);
1035
1036	/* done building the range table and jointree */
1037	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
1038	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
1039	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, NULL);
1040
1041	qry->hasTargetSRFs = pstate->p_hasTargetSRFs;
1042	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
1043
1044	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
1045
1046	return qry;
1047}
1048
1049/*
1050 * Prepare an INSERT row for assignment to the target table.
1051 *
1052 * exprlist: transformed expressions for source values; these might come from
1053 * a VALUES row, or be Vars referencing a sub-SELECT or VALUES RTE output.
1054 * stmtcols: original target-columns spec for INSERT (we just test for NIL)
1055 * icolumns: effective target-columns spec (list of ResTarget)
1056 * attrnos: integer column numbers (must be same length as icolumns)
1057 * strip_indirection: if true, remove any field/array assignment nodes
1058 */
1059List *
1060transformInsertRow(ParseState *pstate, List *exprlist,
1061				   List *stmtcols, List *icolumns, List *attrnos,
1062				   bool strip_indirection)
1063{
1064	List	   *result;
1065	ListCell   *lc;
1066	ListCell   *icols;
1067	ListCell   *attnos;
1068
1069	/*
1070	 * Check length of expr list.  It must not have more expressions than
1071	 * there are target columns.  We allow fewer, but only if no explicit
1072	 * columns list was given (the remaining columns are implicitly
1073	 * defaulted).  Note we must check this *after* transformation because
1074	 * that could expand '*' into multiple items.
1075	 */
1076	if (list_length(exprlist) > list_length(icolumns))
1077		ereport(ERROR,
1078				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
1079				 errmsg("INSERT has more expressions than target columns"),
1080				 parser_errposition(pstate,
1081									exprLocation(list_nth(exprlist,
1082														  list_length(icolumns))))));
1083	if (stmtcols != NIL &&
1084		list_length(exprlist) < list_length(icolumns))
1085	{
1086		/*
1087		 * We can get here for cases like INSERT ... SELECT (a,b,c) FROM ...
1088		 * where the user accidentally created a RowExpr instead of separate
1089		 * columns.  Add a suitable hint if that seems to be the problem,
1090		 * because the main error message is quite misleading for this case.
1091		 * (If there's no stmtcols, you'll get something about data type
1092		 * mismatch, which is less misleading so we don't worry about giving a
1093		 * hint in that case.)
1094		 */
1095		ereport(ERROR,
1096				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
1097				 errmsg("INSERT has more target columns than expressions"),
1098				 ((list_length(exprlist) == 1 &&
1099				   count_rowexpr_columns(pstate, linitial(exprlist)) ==
1100				   list_length(icolumns)) ?
1101				  errhint("The insertion source is a row expression containing the same number of columns expected by the INSERT. Did you accidentally use extra parentheses?") : 0),
1102				 parser_errposition(pstate,
1103									exprLocation(list_nth(icolumns,
1104														  list_length(exprlist))))));
1105	}
1106
1107	/*
1108	 * Prepare columns for assignment to target table.
1109	 */
1110	result = NIL;
1111	forthree(lc, exprlist, icols, icolumns, attnos, attrnos)
1112	{
1113		Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) lfirst(lc);
1114		ResTarget  *col = lfirst_node(ResTarget, icols);
1115		int			attno = lfirst_int(attnos);
1116
1117		expr = transformAssignedExpr(pstate, expr,
1118									 EXPR_KIND_INSERT_TARGET,
1119									 col->name,
1120									 attno,
1121									 col->indirection,
1122									 col->location);
1123
1124		if (strip_indirection)
1125		{
1126			/*
1127			 * We need to remove top-level FieldStores and SubscriptingRefs,
1128			 * as well as any CoerceToDomain appearing above one of those ---
1129			 * but not a CoerceToDomain that isn't above one of those.
1130			 */
1131			while (expr)
1132			{
1133				Expr	   *subexpr = expr;
1134
1135				while (IsA(subexpr, CoerceToDomain))
1136				{
1137					subexpr = ((CoerceToDomain *) subexpr)->arg;
1138				}
1139				if (IsA(subexpr, FieldStore))
1140				{
1141					FieldStore *fstore = (FieldStore *) subexpr;
1142
1143					expr = (Expr *) linitial(fstore->newvals);
1144				}
1145				else if (IsA(subexpr, SubscriptingRef))
1146				{
1147					SubscriptingRef *sbsref = (SubscriptingRef *) subexpr;
1148
1149					if (sbsref->refassgnexpr == NULL)
1150						break;
1151
1152					expr = sbsref->refassgnexpr;
1153				}
1154				else
1155					break;
1156			}
1157		}
1158
1159		result = lappend(result, expr);
1160	}
1161
1162	return result;
1163}
1164
1165/*
1166 * transformOnConflictClause -
1167 *	  transforms an OnConflictClause in an INSERT
1168 */
1169static OnConflictExpr *
1170transformOnConflictClause(ParseState *pstate,
1171						  OnConflictClause *onConflictClause)
1172{
1173	ParseNamespaceItem *exclNSItem = NULL;
1174	List	   *arbiterElems;
1175	Node	   *arbiterWhere;
1176	Oid			arbiterConstraint;
1177	List	   *onConflictSet = NIL;
1178	Node	   *onConflictWhere = NULL;
1179	int			exclRelIndex = 0;
1180	List	   *exclRelTlist = NIL;
1181	OnConflictExpr *result;
1182
1183	/*
1184	 * If this is ON CONFLICT ... UPDATE, first create the range table entry
1185	 * for the EXCLUDED pseudo relation, so that that will be present while
1186	 * processing arbiter expressions.  (You can't actually reference it from
1187	 * there, but this provides a useful error message if you try.)
1188	 */
1189	if (onConflictClause->action == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE)
1190	{
1191		Relation	targetrel = pstate->p_target_relation;
1192		RangeTblEntry *exclRte;
1193
1194		exclNSItem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate,
1195												   targetrel,
1196												   RowExclusiveLock,
1197												   makeAlias("excluded", NIL),
1198												   false, false);
1199		exclRte = exclNSItem->p_rte;
1200		exclRelIndex = exclNSItem->p_rtindex;
1201
1202		/*
1203		 * relkind is set to composite to signal that we're not dealing with
1204		 * an actual relation, and no permission checks are required on it.
1205		 * (We'll check the actual target relation, instead.)
1206		 */
1207		exclRte->relkind = RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE;
1208
1209		/* Create EXCLUDED rel's targetlist for use by EXPLAIN */
1210		exclRelTlist = BuildOnConflictExcludedTargetlist(targetrel,
1211														 exclRelIndex);
1212	}
1213
1214	/* Process the arbiter clause, ON CONFLICT ON (...) */
1215	transformOnConflictArbiter(pstate, onConflictClause, &arbiterElems,
1216							   &arbiterWhere, &arbiterConstraint);
1217
1218	/* Process DO UPDATE */
1219	if (onConflictClause->action == ONCONFLICT_UPDATE)
1220	{
1221		/*
1222		 * Expressions in the UPDATE targetlist need to be handled like UPDATE
1223		 * not INSERT.  We don't need to save/restore this because all INSERT
1224		 * expressions have been parsed already.
1225		 */
1226		pstate->p_is_insert = false;
1227
1228		/*
1229		 * Add the EXCLUDED pseudo relation to the query namespace, making it
1230		 * available in the UPDATE subexpressions.
1231		 */
1232		addNSItemToQuery(pstate, exclNSItem, false, true, true);
1233
1234		/*
1235		 * Now transform the UPDATE subexpressions.
1236		 */
1237		onConflictSet =
1238			transformUpdateTargetList(pstate, onConflictClause->targetList);
1239
1240		onConflictWhere = transformWhereClause(pstate,
1241											   onConflictClause->whereClause,
1242											   EXPR_KIND_WHERE, "WHERE");
1243
1244		/*
1245		 * Remove the EXCLUDED pseudo relation from the query namespace, since
1246		 * it's not supposed to be available in RETURNING.  (Maybe someday we
1247		 * could allow that, and drop this step.)
1248		 */
1249		Assert((ParseNamespaceItem *) llast(pstate->p_namespace) == exclNSItem);
1250		pstate->p_namespace = list_delete_last(pstate->p_namespace);
1251	}
1252
1253	/* Finally, build ON CONFLICT DO [NOTHING | UPDATE] expression */
1254	result = makeNode(OnConflictExpr);
1255
1256	result->action = onConflictClause->action;
1257	result->arbiterElems = arbiterElems;
1258	result->arbiterWhere = arbiterWhere;
1259	result->constraint = arbiterConstraint;
1260	result->onConflictSet = onConflictSet;
1261	result->onConflictWhere = onConflictWhere;
1262	result->exclRelIndex = exclRelIndex;
1263	result->exclRelTlist = exclRelTlist;
1264
1265	return result;
1266}
1267
1268
1269/*
1270 * BuildOnConflictExcludedTargetlist
1271 *		Create target list for the EXCLUDED pseudo-relation of ON CONFLICT,
1272 *		representing the columns of targetrel with varno exclRelIndex.
1273 *
1274 * Note: Exported for use in the rewriter.
1275 */
1276List *
1277BuildOnConflictExcludedTargetlist(Relation targetrel,
1278								  Index exclRelIndex)
1279{
1280	List	   *result = NIL;
1281	int			attno;
1282	Var		   *var;
1283	TargetEntry *te;
1284
1285	/*
1286	 * Note that resnos of the tlist must correspond to attnos of the
1287	 * underlying relation, hence we need entries for dropped columns too.
1288	 */
1289	for (attno = 0; attno < RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(targetrel); attno++)
1290	{
1291		Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(targetrel->rd_att, attno);
1292		char	   *name;
1293
1294		if (attr->attisdropped)
1295		{
1296			/*
1297			 * can't use atttypid here, but it doesn't really matter what type
1298			 * the Const claims to be.
1299			 */
1300			var = (Var *) makeNullConst(INT4OID, -1, InvalidOid);
1301			name = NULL;
1302		}
1303		else
1304		{
1305			var = makeVar(exclRelIndex, attno + 1,
1306						  attr->atttypid, attr->atttypmod,
1307						  attr->attcollation,
1308						  0);
1309			name = pstrdup(NameStr(attr->attname));
1310		}
1311
1312		te = makeTargetEntry((Expr *) var,
1313							 attno + 1,
1314							 name,
1315							 false);
1316
1317		result = lappend(result, te);
1318	}
1319
1320	/*
1321	 * Add a whole-row-Var entry to support references to "EXCLUDED.*".  Like
1322	 * the other entries in the EXCLUDED tlist, its resno must match the Var's
1323	 * varattno, else the wrong things happen while resolving references in
1324	 * setrefs.c.  This is against normal conventions for targetlists, but
1325	 * it's okay since we don't use this as a real tlist.
1326	 */
1327	var = makeVar(exclRelIndex, InvalidAttrNumber,
1328				  targetrel->rd_rel->reltype,
1329				  -1, InvalidOid, 0);
1330	te = makeTargetEntry((Expr *) var, InvalidAttrNumber, NULL, true);
1331	result = lappend(result, te);
1332
1333	return result;
1334}
1335
1336
1337/*
1338 * count_rowexpr_columns -
1339 *	  get number of columns contained in a ROW() expression;
1340 *	  return -1 if expression isn't a RowExpr or a Var referencing one.
1341 *
1342 * This is currently used only for hint purposes, so we aren't terribly
1343 * tense about recognizing all possible cases.  The Var case is interesting
1344 * because that's what we'll get in the INSERT ... SELECT (...) case.
1345 */
1346static int
1347count_rowexpr_columns(ParseState *pstate, Node *expr)
1348{
1349	if (expr == NULL)
1350		return -1;
1351	if (IsA(expr, RowExpr))
1352		return list_length(((RowExpr *) expr)->args);
1353	if (IsA(expr, Var))
1354	{
1355		Var		   *var = (Var *) expr;
1356		AttrNumber	attnum = var->varattno;
1357
1358		if (attnum > 0 && var->vartype == RECORDOID)
1359		{
1360			RangeTblEntry *rte;
1361
1362			rte = GetRTEByRangeTablePosn(pstate, var->varno, var->varlevelsup);
1363			if (rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY)
1364			{
1365				/* Subselect-in-FROM: examine sub-select's output expr */
1366				TargetEntry *ste = get_tle_by_resno(rte->subquery->targetList,
1367													attnum);
1368
1369				if (ste == NULL || ste->resjunk)
1370					return -1;
1371				expr = (Node *) ste->expr;
1372				if (IsA(expr, RowExpr))
1373					return list_length(((RowExpr *) expr)->args);
1374			}
1375		}
1376	}
1377	return -1;
1378}
1379
1380
1381/*
1382 * transformSelectStmt -
1383 *	  transforms a Select Statement
1384 *
1385 * Note: this covers only cases with no set operations and no VALUES lists;
1386 * see below for the other cases.
1387 */
1388static Query *
1389transformSelectStmt(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt)
1390{
1391	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
1392	Node	   *qual;
1393	ListCell   *l;
1394
1395	qry->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
1396
1397	/* process the WITH clause independently of all else */
1398	if (stmt->withClause)
1399	{
1400		qry->hasRecursive = stmt->withClause->recursive;
1401		qry->cteList = transformWithClause(pstate, stmt->withClause);
1402		qry->hasModifyingCTE = pstate->p_hasModifyingCTE;
1403	}
1404
1405	/* Complain if we get called from someplace where INTO is not allowed */
1406	if (stmt->intoClause)
1407		ereport(ERROR,
1408				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
1409				 errmsg("SELECT ... INTO is not allowed here"),
1410				 parser_errposition(pstate,
1411									exprLocation((Node *) stmt->intoClause))));
1412
1413	/* make FOR UPDATE/FOR SHARE info available to addRangeTableEntry */
1414	pstate->p_locking_clause = stmt->lockingClause;
1415
1416	/* make WINDOW info available for window functions, too */
1417	pstate->p_windowdefs = stmt->windowClause;
1418
1419	/* process the FROM clause */
1420	transformFromClause(pstate, stmt->fromClause);
1421
1422	/* transform targetlist */
1423	qry->targetList = transformTargetList(pstate, stmt->targetList,
1424										  EXPR_KIND_SELECT_TARGET);
1425
1426	/* mark column origins */
1427	markTargetListOrigins(pstate, qry->targetList);
1428
1429	/* transform WHERE */
1430	qual = transformWhereClause(pstate, stmt->whereClause,
1431								EXPR_KIND_WHERE, "WHERE");
1432
1433	/* initial processing of HAVING clause is much like WHERE clause */
1434	qry->havingQual = transformWhereClause(pstate, stmt->havingClause,
1435										   EXPR_KIND_HAVING, "HAVING");
1436
1437	/*
1438	 * Transform sorting/grouping stuff.  Do ORDER BY first because both
1439	 * transformGroupClause and transformDistinctClause need the results. Note
1440	 * that these functions can also change the targetList, so it's passed to
1441	 * them by reference.
1442	 */
1443	qry->sortClause = transformSortClause(pstate,
1444										  stmt->sortClause,
1445										  &qry->targetList,
1446										  EXPR_KIND_ORDER_BY,
1447										  false /* allow SQL92 rules */ );
1448
1449	qry->groupClause = transformGroupClause(pstate,
1450											stmt->groupClause,
1451											&qry->groupingSets,
1452											&qry->targetList,
1453											qry->sortClause,
1454											EXPR_KIND_GROUP_BY,
1455											false /* allow SQL92 rules */ );
1456	qry->groupDistinct = stmt->groupDistinct;
1457
1458	if (stmt->distinctClause == NIL)
1459	{
1460		qry->distinctClause = NIL;
1461		qry->hasDistinctOn = false;
1462	}
1463	else if (linitial(stmt->distinctClause) == NULL)
1464	{
1465		/* We had SELECT DISTINCT */
1466		qry->distinctClause = transformDistinctClause(pstate,
1467													  &qry->targetList,
1468													  qry->sortClause,
1469													  false);
1470		qry->hasDistinctOn = false;
1471	}
1472	else
1473	{
1474		/* We had SELECT DISTINCT ON */
1475		qry->distinctClause = transformDistinctOnClause(pstate,
1476														stmt->distinctClause,
1477														&qry->targetList,
1478														qry->sortClause);
1479		qry->hasDistinctOn = true;
1480	}
1481
1482	/* transform LIMIT */
1483	qry->limitOffset = transformLimitClause(pstate, stmt->limitOffset,
1484											EXPR_KIND_OFFSET, "OFFSET",
1485											stmt->limitOption);
1486	qry->limitCount = transformLimitClause(pstate, stmt->limitCount,
1487										   EXPR_KIND_LIMIT, "LIMIT",
1488										   stmt->limitOption);
1489	qry->limitOption = stmt->limitOption;
1490
1491	/* transform window clauses after we have seen all window functions */
1492	qry->windowClause = transformWindowDefinitions(pstate,
1493												   pstate->p_windowdefs,
1494												   &qry->targetList);
1495
1496	/* resolve any still-unresolved output columns as being type text */
1497	if (pstate->p_resolve_unknowns)
1498		resolveTargetListUnknowns(pstate, qry->targetList);
1499
1500	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
1501	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
1502	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, qual);
1503
1504	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
1505	qry->hasWindowFuncs = pstate->p_hasWindowFuncs;
1506	qry->hasTargetSRFs = pstate->p_hasTargetSRFs;
1507	qry->hasAggs = pstate->p_hasAggs;
1508
1509	foreach(l, stmt->lockingClause)
1510	{
1511		transformLockingClause(pstate, qry,
1512							   (LockingClause *) lfirst(l), false);
1513	}
1514
1515	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
1516
1517	/* this must be done after collations, for reliable comparison of exprs */
1518	if (pstate->p_hasAggs || qry->groupClause || qry->groupingSets || qry->havingQual)
1519		parseCheckAggregates(pstate, qry);
1520
1521	return qry;
1522}
1523
1524/*
1525 * transformValuesClause -
1526 *	  transforms a VALUES clause that's being used as a standalone SELECT
1527 *
1528 * We build a Query containing a VALUES RTE, rather as if one had written
1529 *			SELECT * FROM (VALUES ...) AS "*VALUES*"
1530 */
1531static Query *
1532transformValuesClause(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt)
1533{
1534	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
1535	List	   *exprsLists = NIL;
1536	List	   *coltypes = NIL;
1537	List	   *coltypmods = NIL;
1538	List	   *colcollations = NIL;
1539	List	  **colexprs = NULL;
1540	int			sublist_length = -1;
1541	bool		lateral = false;
1542	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
1543	ListCell   *lc;
1544	ListCell   *lc2;
1545	int			i;
1546
1547	qry->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
1548
1549	/* Most SELECT stuff doesn't apply in a VALUES clause */
1550	Assert(stmt->distinctClause == NIL);
1551	Assert(stmt->intoClause == NULL);
1552	Assert(stmt->targetList == NIL);
1553	Assert(stmt->fromClause == NIL);
1554	Assert(stmt->whereClause == NULL);
1555	Assert(stmt->groupClause == NIL);
1556	Assert(stmt->havingClause == NULL);
1557	Assert(stmt->windowClause == NIL);
1558	Assert(stmt->op == SETOP_NONE);
1559
1560	/* process the WITH clause independently of all else */
1561	if (stmt->withClause)
1562	{
1563		qry->hasRecursive = stmt->withClause->recursive;
1564		qry->cteList = transformWithClause(pstate, stmt->withClause);
1565		qry->hasModifyingCTE = pstate->p_hasModifyingCTE;
1566	}
1567
1568	/*
1569	 * For each row of VALUES, transform the raw expressions.
1570	 *
1571	 * Note that the intermediate representation we build is column-organized
1572	 * not row-organized.  That simplifies the type and collation processing
1573	 * below.
1574	 */
1575	foreach(lc, stmt->valuesLists)
1576	{
1577		List	   *sublist = (List *) lfirst(lc);
1578
1579		/*
1580		 * Do basic expression transformation (same as a ROW() expr, but here
1581		 * we disallow SetToDefault)
1582		 */
1583		sublist = transformExpressionList(pstate, sublist,
1584										  EXPR_KIND_VALUES, false);
1585
1586		/*
1587		 * All the sublists must be the same length, *after* transformation
1588		 * (which might expand '*' into multiple items).  The VALUES RTE can't
1589		 * handle anything different.
1590		 */
1591		if (sublist_length < 0)
1592		{
1593			/* Remember post-transformation length of first sublist */
1594			sublist_length = list_length(sublist);
1595			/* and allocate array for per-column lists */
1596			colexprs = (List **) palloc0(sublist_length * sizeof(List *));
1597		}
1598		else if (sublist_length != list_length(sublist))
1599		{
1600			ereport(ERROR,
1601					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
1602					 errmsg("VALUES lists must all be the same length"),
1603					 parser_errposition(pstate,
1604										exprLocation((Node *) sublist))));
1605		}
1606
1607		/* Build per-column expression lists */
1608		i = 0;
1609		foreach(lc2, sublist)
1610		{
1611			Node	   *col = (Node *) lfirst(lc2);
1612
1613			colexprs[i] = lappend(colexprs[i], col);
1614			i++;
1615		}
1616
1617		/* Release sub-list's cells to save memory */
1618		list_free(sublist);
1619
1620		/* Prepare an exprsLists element for this row */
1621		exprsLists = lappend(exprsLists, NIL);
1622	}
1623
1624	/*
1625	 * Now resolve the common types of the columns, and coerce everything to
1626	 * those types.  Then identify the common typmod and common collation, if
1627	 * any, of each column.
1628	 *
1629	 * We must do collation processing now because (1) assign_query_collations
1630	 * doesn't process rangetable entries, and (2) we need to label the VALUES
1631	 * RTE with column collations for use in the outer query.  We don't
1632	 * consider conflict of implicit collations to be an error here; instead
1633	 * the column will just show InvalidOid as its collation, and you'll get a
1634	 * failure later if that results in failure to resolve a collation.
1635	 *
1636	 * Note we modify the per-column expression lists in-place.
1637	 */
1638	for (i = 0; i < sublist_length; i++)
1639	{
1640		Oid			coltype;
1641		int32		coltypmod;
1642		Oid			colcoll;
1643
1644		coltype = select_common_type(pstate, colexprs[i], "VALUES", NULL);
1645
1646		foreach(lc, colexprs[i])
1647		{
1648			Node	   *col = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
1649
1650			col = coerce_to_common_type(pstate, col, coltype, "VALUES");
1651			lfirst(lc) = col;
1652		}
1653
1654		coltypmod = select_common_typmod(pstate, colexprs[i], coltype);
1655		colcoll = select_common_collation(pstate, colexprs[i], true);
1656
1657		coltypes = lappend_oid(coltypes, coltype);
1658		coltypmods = lappend_int(coltypmods, coltypmod);
1659		colcollations = lappend_oid(colcollations, colcoll);
1660	}
1661
1662	/*
1663	 * Finally, rearrange the coerced expressions into row-organized lists.
1664	 */
1665	for (i = 0; i < sublist_length; i++)
1666	{
1667		forboth(lc, colexprs[i], lc2, exprsLists)
1668		{
1669			Node	   *col = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
1670			List	   *sublist = lfirst(lc2);
1671
1672			sublist = lappend(sublist, col);
1673			lfirst(lc2) = sublist;
1674		}
1675		list_free(colexprs[i]);
1676	}
1677
1678	/*
1679	 * Ordinarily there can't be any current-level Vars in the expression
1680	 * lists, because the namespace was empty ... but if we're inside CREATE
1681	 * RULE, then NEW/OLD references might appear.  In that case we have to
1682	 * mark the VALUES RTE as LATERAL.
1683	 */
1684	if (pstate->p_rtable != NIL &&
1685		contain_vars_of_level((Node *) exprsLists, 0))
1686		lateral = true;
1687
1688	/*
1689	 * Generate the VALUES RTE
1690	 */
1691	nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForValues(pstate, exprsLists,
1692										 coltypes, coltypmods, colcollations,
1693										 NULL, lateral, true);
1694	addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, true, true, true);
1695
1696	/*
1697	 * Generate a targetlist as though expanding "*"
1698	 */
1699	Assert(pstate->p_next_resno == 1);
1700	qry->targetList = expandNSItemAttrs(pstate, nsitem, 0, true, -1);
1701
1702	/*
1703	 * The grammar allows attaching ORDER BY, LIMIT, and FOR UPDATE to a
1704	 * VALUES, so cope.
1705	 */
1706	qry->sortClause = transformSortClause(pstate,
1707										  stmt->sortClause,
1708										  &qry->targetList,
1709										  EXPR_KIND_ORDER_BY,
1710										  false /* allow SQL92 rules */ );
1711
1712	qry->limitOffset = transformLimitClause(pstate, stmt->limitOffset,
1713											EXPR_KIND_OFFSET, "OFFSET",
1714											stmt->limitOption);
1715	qry->limitCount = transformLimitClause(pstate, stmt->limitCount,
1716										   EXPR_KIND_LIMIT, "LIMIT",
1717										   stmt->limitOption);
1718	qry->limitOption = stmt->limitOption;
1719
1720	if (stmt->lockingClause)
1721		ereport(ERROR,
1722				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1723		/*------
1724		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
1725				 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to VALUES",
1726						LCS_asString(((LockingClause *)
1727									  linitial(stmt->lockingClause))->strength))));
1728
1729	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
1730	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
1731	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, NULL);
1732
1733	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
1734
1735	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
1736
1737	return qry;
1738}
1739
1740/*
1741 * transformSetOperationStmt -
1742 *	  transforms a set-operations tree
1743 *
1744 * A set-operation tree is just a SELECT, but with UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT
1745 * structure to it.  We must transform each leaf SELECT and build up a top-
1746 * level Query that contains the leaf SELECTs as subqueries in its rangetable.
1747 * The tree of set operations is converted into the setOperations field of
1748 * the top-level Query.
1749 */
1750static Query *
1751transformSetOperationStmt(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt)
1752{
1753	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
1754	SelectStmt *leftmostSelect;
1755	int			leftmostRTI;
1756	Query	   *leftmostQuery;
1757	SetOperationStmt *sostmt;
1758	List	   *sortClause;
1759	Node	   *limitOffset;
1760	Node	   *limitCount;
1761	List	   *lockingClause;
1762	WithClause *withClause;
1763	Node	   *node;
1764	ListCell   *left_tlist,
1765			   *lct,
1766			   *lcm,
1767			   *lcc,
1768			   *l;
1769	List	   *targetvars,
1770			   *targetnames,
1771			   *sv_namespace;
1772	int			sv_rtable_length;
1773	ParseNamespaceItem *jnsitem;
1774	ParseNamespaceColumn *sortnscolumns;
1775	int			sortcolindex;
1776	int			tllen;
1777
1778	qry->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
1779
1780	/*
1781	 * Find leftmost leaf SelectStmt.  We currently only need to do this in
1782	 * order to deliver a suitable error message if there's an INTO clause
1783	 * there, implying the set-op tree is in a context that doesn't allow
1784	 * INTO.  (transformSetOperationTree would throw error anyway, but it
1785	 * seems worth the trouble to throw a different error for non-leftmost
1786	 * INTO, so we produce that error in transformSetOperationTree.)
1787	 */
1788	leftmostSelect = stmt->larg;
1789	while (leftmostSelect && leftmostSelect->op != SETOP_NONE)
1790		leftmostSelect = leftmostSelect->larg;
1791	Assert(leftmostSelect && IsA(leftmostSelect, SelectStmt) &&
1792		   leftmostSelect->larg == NULL);
1793	if (leftmostSelect->intoClause)
1794		ereport(ERROR,
1795				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
1796				 errmsg("SELECT ... INTO is not allowed here"),
1797				 parser_errposition(pstate,
1798									exprLocation((Node *) leftmostSelect->intoClause))));
1799
1800	/*
1801	 * We need to extract ORDER BY and other top-level clauses here and not
1802	 * let transformSetOperationTree() see them --- else it'll just recurse
1803	 * right back here!
1804	 */
1805	sortClause = stmt->sortClause;
1806	limitOffset = stmt->limitOffset;
1807	limitCount = stmt->limitCount;
1808	lockingClause = stmt->lockingClause;
1809	withClause = stmt->withClause;
1810
1811	stmt->sortClause = NIL;
1812	stmt->limitOffset = NULL;
1813	stmt->limitCount = NULL;
1814	stmt->lockingClause = NIL;
1815	stmt->withClause = NULL;
1816
1817	/* We don't support FOR UPDATE/SHARE with set ops at the moment. */
1818	if (lockingClause)
1819		ereport(ERROR,
1820				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1821		/*------
1822		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
1823				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT",
1824						LCS_asString(((LockingClause *)
1825									  linitial(lockingClause))->strength))));
1826
1827	/* Process the WITH clause independently of all else */
1828	if (withClause)
1829	{
1830		qry->hasRecursive = withClause->recursive;
1831		qry->cteList = transformWithClause(pstate, withClause);
1832		qry->hasModifyingCTE = pstate->p_hasModifyingCTE;
1833	}
1834
1835	/*
1836	 * Recursively transform the components of the tree.
1837	 */
1838	sostmt = castNode(SetOperationStmt,
1839					  transformSetOperationTree(pstate, stmt, true, NULL));
1840	Assert(sostmt);
1841	qry->setOperations = (Node *) sostmt;
1842
1843	/*
1844	 * Re-find leftmost SELECT (now it's a sub-query in rangetable)
1845	 */
1846	node = sostmt->larg;
1847	while (node && IsA(node, SetOperationStmt))
1848		node = ((SetOperationStmt *) node)->larg;
1849	Assert(node && IsA(node, RangeTblRef));
1850	leftmostRTI = ((RangeTblRef *) node)->rtindex;
1851	leftmostQuery = rt_fetch(leftmostRTI, pstate->p_rtable)->subquery;
1852	Assert(leftmostQuery != NULL);
1853
1854	/*
1855	 * Generate dummy targetlist for outer query using column names of
1856	 * leftmost select and common datatypes/collations of topmost set
1857	 * operation.  Also make lists of the dummy vars and their names for use
1858	 * in parsing ORDER BY.
1859	 *
1860	 * Note: we use leftmostRTI as the varno of the dummy variables. It
1861	 * shouldn't matter too much which RT index they have, as long as they
1862	 * have one that corresponds to a real RT entry; else funny things may
1863	 * happen when the tree is mashed by rule rewriting.
1864	 */
1865	qry->targetList = NIL;
1866	targetvars = NIL;
1867	targetnames = NIL;
1868	sortnscolumns = (ParseNamespaceColumn *)
1869		palloc0(list_length(sostmt->colTypes) * sizeof(ParseNamespaceColumn));
1870	sortcolindex = 0;
1871
1872	forfour(lct, sostmt->colTypes,
1873			lcm, sostmt->colTypmods,
1874			lcc, sostmt->colCollations,
1875			left_tlist, leftmostQuery->targetList)
1876	{
1877		Oid			colType = lfirst_oid(lct);
1878		int32		colTypmod = lfirst_int(lcm);
1879		Oid			colCollation = lfirst_oid(lcc);
1880		TargetEntry *lefttle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(left_tlist);
1881		char	   *colName;
1882		TargetEntry *tle;
1883		Var		   *var;
1884
1885		Assert(!lefttle->resjunk);
1886		colName = pstrdup(lefttle->resname);
1887		var = makeVar(leftmostRTI,
1888					  lefttle->resno,
1889					  colType,
1890					  colTypmod,
1891					  colCollation,
1892					  0);
1893		var->location = exprLocation((Node *) lefttle->expr);
1894		tle = makeTargetEntry((Expr *) var,
1895							  (AttrNumber) pstate->p_next_resno++,
1896							  colName,
1897							  false);
1898		qry->targetList = lappend(qry->targetList, tle);
1899		targetvars = lappend(targetvars, var);
1900		targetnames = lappend(targetnames, makeString(colName));
1901		sortnscolumns[sortcolindex].p_varno = leftmostRTI;
1902		sortnscolumns[sortcolindex].p_varattno = lefttle->resno;
1903		sortnscolumns[sortcolindex].p_vartype = colType;
1904		sortnscolumns[sortcolindex].p_vartypmod = colTypmod;
1905		sortnscolumns[sortcolindex].p_varcollid = colCollation;
1906		sortnscolumns[sortcolindex].p_varnosyn = leftmostRTI;
1907		sortnscolumns[sortcolindex].p_varattnosyn = lefttle->resno;
1908		sortcolindex++;
1909	}
1910
1911	/*
1912	 * As a first step towards supporting sort clauses that are expressions
1913	 * using the output columns, generate a namespace entry that makes the
1914	 * output columns visible.  A Join RTE node is handy for this, since we
1915	 * can easily control the Vars generated upon matches.
1916	 *
1917	 * Note: we don't yet do anything useful with such cases, but at least
1918	 * "ORDER BY upper(foo)" will draw the right error message rather than
1919	 * "foo not found".
1920	 */
1921	sv_rtable_length = list_length(pstate->p_rtable);
1922
1923	jnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForJoin(pstate,
1924										targetnames,
1925										sortnscolumns,
1926										JOIN_INNER,
1927										0,
1928										targetvars,
1929										NIL,
1930										NIL,
1931										NULL,
1932										NULL,
1933										false);
1934
1935	sv_namespace = pstate->p_namespace;
1936	pstate->p_namespace = NIL;
1937
1938	/* add jnsitem to column namespace only */
1939	addNSItemToQuery(pstate, jnsitem, false, false, true);
1940
1941	/*
1942	 * For now, we don't support resjunk sort clauses on the output of a
1943	 * setOperation tree --- you can only use the SQL92-spec options of
1944	 * selecting an output column by name or number.  Enforce by checking that
1945	 * transformSortClause doesn't add any items to tlist.  Note, if changing
1946	 * this, add_setop_child_rel_equivalences() will need to be updated.
1947	 */
1948	tllen = list_length(qry->targetList);
1949
1950	qry->sortClause = transformSortClause(pstate,
1951										  sortClause,
1952										  &qry->targetList,
1953										  EXPR_KIND_ORDER_BY,
1954										  false /* allow SQL92 rules */ );
1955
1956	/* restore namespace, remove join RTE from rtable */
1957	pstate->p_namespace = sv_namespace;
1958	pstate->p_rtable = list_truncate(pstate->p_rtable, sv_rtable_length);
1959
1960	if (tllen != list_length(qry->targetList))
1961		ereport(ERROR,
1962				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1963				 errmsg("invalid UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT ORDER BY clause"),
1964				 errdetail("Only result column names can be used, not expressions or functions."),
1965				 errhint("Add the expression/function to every SELECT, or move the UNION into a FROM clause."),
1966				 parser_errposition(pstate,
1967									exprLocation(list_nth(qry->targetList, tllen)))));
1968
1969	qry->limitOffset = transformLimitClause(pstate, limitOffset,
1970											EXPR_KIND_OFFSET, "OFFSET",
1971											stmt->limitOption);
1972	qry->limitCount = transformLimitClause(pstate, limitCount,
1973										   EXPR_KIND_LIMIT, "LIMIT",
1974										   stmt->limitOption);
1975	qry->limitOption = stmt->limitOption;
1976
1977	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
1978	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
1979	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, NULL);
1980
1981	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
1982	qry->hasWindowFuncs = pstate->p_hasWindowFuncs;
1983	qry->hasTargetSRFs = pstate->p_hasTargetSRFs;
1984	qry->hasAggs = pstate->p_hasAggs;
1985
1986	foreach(l, lockingClause)
1987	{
1988		transformLockingClause(pstate, qry,
1989							   (LockingClause *) lfirst(l), false);
1990	}
1991
1992	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
1993
1994	/* this must be done after collations, for reliable comparison of exprs */
1995	if (pstate->p_hasAggs || qry->groupClause || qry->groupingSets || qry->havingQual)
1996		parseCheckAggregates(pstate, qry);
1997
1998	return qry;
1999}
2000
2001/*
2002 * Make a SortGroupClause node for a SetOperationStmt's groupClauses
2003 *
2004 * If require_hash is true, the caller is indicating that they need hash
2005 * support or they will fail.  So look extra hard for hash support.
2006 */
2007SortGroupClause *
2008makeSortGroupClauseForSetOp(Oid rescoltype, bool require_hash)
2009{
2010	SortGroupClause *grpcl = makeNode(SortGroupClause);
2011	Oid			sortop;
2012	Oid			eqop;
2013	bool		hashable;
2014
2015	/* determine the eqop and optional sortop */
2016	get_sort_group_operators(rescoltype,
2017							 false, true, false,
2018							 &sortop, &eqop, NULL,
2019							 &hashable);
2020
2021	/*
2022	 * The type cache doesn't believe that record is hashable (see
2023	 * cache_record_field_properties()), but if the caller really needs hash
2024	 * support, we can assume it does.  Worst case, if any components of the
2025	 * record don't support hashing, we will fail at execution.
2026	 */
2027	if (require_hash && (rescoltype == RECORDOID || rescoltype == RECORDARRAYOID))
2028		hashable = true;
2029
2030	/* we don't have a tlist yet, so can't assign sortgrouprefs */
2031	grpcl->tleSortGroupRef = 0;
2032	grpcl->eqop = eqop;
2033	grpcl->sortop = sortop;
2034	grpcl->reverse_sort = false;	/* Sort-op is "less than", or InvalidOid */
2035	grpcl->nulls_first = false; /* OK with or without sortop */
2036	grpcl->hashable = hashable;
2037
2038	return grpcl;
2039}
2040
2041/*
2042 * transformSetOperationTree
2043 *		Recursively transform leaves and internal nodes of a set-op tree
2044 *
2045 * In addition to returning the transformed node, if targetlist isn't NULL
2046 * then we return a list of its non-resjunk TargetEntry nodes.  For a leaf
2047 * set-op node these are the actual targetlist entries; otherwise they are
2048 * dummy entries created to carry the type, typmod, collation, and location
2049 * (for error messages) of each output column of the set-op node.  This info
2050 * is needed only during the internal recursion of this function, so outside
2051 * callers pass NULL for targetlist.  Note: the reason for passing the
2052 * actual targetlist entries of a leaf node is so that upper levels can
2053 * replace UNKNOWN Consts with properly-coerced constants.
2054 */
2055static Node *
2056transformSetOperationTree(ParseState *pstate, SelectStmt *stmt,
2057						  bool isTopLevel, List **targetlist)
2058{
2059	bool		isLeaf;
2060
2061	Assert(stmt && IsA(stmt, SelectStmt));
2062
2063	/* Guard against stack overflow due to overly complex set-expressions */
2064	check_stack_depth();
2065
2066	/*
2067	 * Validity-check both leaf and internal SELECTs for disallowed ops.
2068	 */
2069	if (stmt->intoClause)
2070		ereport(ERROR,
2071				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2072				 errmsg("INTO is only allowed on first SELECT of UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT"),
2073				 parser_errposition(pstate,
2074									exprLocation((Node *) stmt->intoClause))));
2075
2076	/* We don't support FOR UPDATE/SHARE with set ops at the moment. */
2077	if (stmt->lockingClause)
2078		ereport(ERROR,
2079				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2080		/*------
2081		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
2082				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT",
2083						LCS_asString(((LockingClause *)
2084									  linitial(stmt->lockingClause))->strength))));
2085
2086	/*
2087	 * If an internal node of a set-op tree has ORDER BY, LIMIT, FOR UPDATE,
2088	 * or WITH clauses attached, we need to treat it like a leaf node to
2089	 * generate an independent sub-Query tree.  Otherwise, it can be
2090	 * represented by a SetOperationStmt node underneath the parent Query.
2091	 */
2092	if (stmt->op == SETOP_NONE)
2093	{
2094		Assert(stmt->larg == NULL && stmt->rarg == NULL);
2095		isLeaf = true;
2096	}
2097	else
2098	{
2099		Assert(stmt->larg != NULL && stmt->rarg != NULL);
2100		if (stmt->sortClause || stmt->limitOffset || stmt->limitCount ||
2101			stmt->lockingClause || stmt->withClause)
2102			isLeaf = true;
2103		else
2104			isLeaf = false;
2105	}
2106
2107	if (isLeaf)
2108	{
2109		/* Process leaf SELECT */
2110		Query	   *selectQuery;
2111		char		selectName[32];
2112		ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
2113		RangeTblRef *rtr;
2114		ListCell   *tl;
2115
2116		/*
2117		 * Transform SelectStmt into a Query.
2118		 *
2119		 * This works the same as SELECT transformation normally would, except
2120		 * that we prevent resolving unknown-type outputs as TEXT.  This does
2121		 * not change the subquery's semantics since if the column type
2122		 * matters semantically, it would have been resolved to something else
2123		 * anyway.  Doing this lets us resolve such outputs using
2124		 * select_common_type(), below.
2125		 *
2126		 * Note: previously transformed sub-queries don't affect the parsing
2127		 * of this sub-query, because they are not in the toplevel pstate's
2128		 * namespace list.
2129		 */
2130		selectQuery = parse_sub_analyze((Node *) stmt, pstate,
2131										NULL, false, false);
2132
2133		/*
2134		 * Check for bogus references to Vars on the current query level (but
2135		 * upper-level references are okay). Normally this can't happen
2136		 * because the namespace will be empty, but it could happen if we are
2137		 * inside a rule.
2138		 */
2139		if (pstate->p_namespace)
2140		{
2141			if (contain_vars_of_level((Node *) selectQuery, 1))
2142				ereport(ERROR,
2143						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),
2144						 errmsg("UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT member statement cannot refer to other relations of same query level"),
2145						 parser_errposition(pstate,
2146											locate_var_of_level((Node *) selectQuery, 1))));
2147		}
2148
2149		/*
2150		 * Extract a list of the non-junk TLEs for upper-level processing.
2151		 */
2152		if (targetlist)
2153		{
2154			*targetlist = NIL;
2155			foreach(tl, selectQuery->targetList)
2156			{
2157				TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(tl);
2158
2159				if (!tle->resjunk)
2160					*targetlist = lappend(*targetlist, tle);
2161			}
2162		}
2163
2164		/*
2165		 * Make the leaf query be a subquery in the top-level rangetable.
2166		 */
2167		snprintf(selectName, sizeof(selectName), "*SELECT* %d",
2168				 list_length(pstate->p_rtable) + 1);
2169		nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForSubquery(pstate,
2170											   selectQuery,
2171											   makeAlias(selectName, NIL),
2172											   false,
2173											   false);
2174
2175		/*
2176		 * Return a RangeTblRef to replace the SelectStmt in the set-op tree.
2177		 */
2178		rtr = makeNode(RangeTblRef);
2179		rtr->rtindex = nsitem->p_rtindex;
2180		return (Node *) rtr;
2181	}
2182	else
2183	{
2184		/* Process an internal node (set operation node) */
2185		SetOperationStmt *op = makeNode(SetOperationStmt);
2186		List	   *ltargetlist;
2187		List	   *rtargetlist;
2188		ListCell   *ltl;
2189		ListCell   *rtl;
2190		const char *context;
2191		bool		recursive = (pstate->p_parent_cte &&
2192								 pstate->p_parent_cte->cterecursive);
2193
2194		context = (stmt->op == SETOP_UNION ? "UNION" :
2195				   (stmt->op == SETOP_INTERSECT ? "INTERSECT" :
2196					"EXCEPT"));
2197
2198		op->op = stmt->op;
2199		op->all = stmt->all;
2200
2201		/*
2202		 * Recursively transform the left child node.
2203		 */
2204		op->larg = transformSetOperationTree(pstate, stmt->larg,
2205											 false,
2206											 &ltargetlist);
2207
2208		/*
2209		 * If we are processing a recursive union query, now is the time to
2210		 * examine the non-recursive term's output columns and mark the
2211		 * containing CTE as having those result columns.  We should do this
2212		 * only at the topmost setop of the CTE, of course.
2213		 */
2214		if (isTopLevel && recursive)
2215			determineRecursiveColTypes(pstate, op->larg, ltargetlist);
2216
2217		/*
2218		 * Recursively transform the right child node.
2219		 */
2220		op->rarg = transformSetOperationTree(pstate, stmt->rarg,
2221											 false,
2222											 &rtargetlist);
2223
2224		/*
2225		 * Verify that the two children have the same number of non-junk
2226		 * columns, and determine the types of the merged output columns.
2227		 */
2228		if (list_length(ltargetlist) != list_length(rtargetlist))
2229			ereport(ERROR,
2230					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2231					 errmsg("each %s query must have the same number of columns",
2232							context),
2233					 parser_errposition(pstate,
2234										exprLocation((Node *) rtargetlist))));
2235
2236		if (targetlist)
2237			*targetlist = NIL;
2238		op->colTypes = NIL;
2239		op->colTypmods = NIL;
2240		op->colCollations = NIL;
2241		op->groupClauses = NIL;
2242		forboth(ltl, ltargetlist, rtl, rtargetlist)
2243		{
2244			TargetEntry *ltle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(ltl);
2245			TargetEntry *rtle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(rtl);
2246			Node	   *lcolnode = (Node *) ltle->expr;
2247			Node	   *rcolnode = (Node *) rtle->expr;
2248			Oid			lcoltype = exprType(lcolnode);
2249			Oid			rcoltype = exprType(rcolnode);
2250			Node	   *bestexpr;
2251			int			bestlocation;
2252			Oid			rescoltype;
2253			int32		rescoltypmod;
2254			Oid			rescolcoll;
2255
2256			/* select common type, same as CASE et al */
2257			rescoltype = select_common_type(pstate,
2258											list_make2(lcolnode, rcolnode),
2259											context,
2260											&bestexpr);
2261			bestlocation = exprLocation(bestexpr);
2262
2263			/*
2264			 * Verify the coercions are actually possible.  If not, we'd fail
2265			 * later anyway, but we want to fail now while we have sufficient
2266			 * context to produce an error cursor position.
2267			 *
2268			 * For all non-UNKNOWN-type cases, we verify coercibility but we
2269			 * don't modify the child's expression, for fear of changing the
2270			 * child query's semantics.
2271			 *
2272			 * If a child expression is an UNKNOWN-type Const or Param, we
2273			 * want to replace it with the coerced expression.  This can only
2274			 * happen when the child is a leaf set-op node.  It's safe to
2275			 * replace the expression because if the child query's semantics
2276			 * depended on the type of this output column, it'd have already
2277			 * coerced the UNKNOWN to something else.  We want to do this
2278			 * because (a) we want to verify that a Const is valid for the
2279			 * target type, or resolve the actual type of an UNKNOWN Param,
2280			 * and (b) we want to avoid unnecessary discrepancies between the
2281			 * output type of the child query and the resolved target type.
2282			 * Such a discrepancy would disable optimization in the planner.
2283			 *
2284			 * If it's some other UNKNOWN-type node, eg a Var, we do nothing
2285			 * (knowing that coerce_to_common_type would fail).  The planner
2286			 * is sometimes able to fold an UNKNOWN Var to a constant before
2287			 * it has to coerce the type, so failing now would just break
2288			 * cases that might work.
2289			 */
2290			if (lcoltype != UNKNOWNOID)
2291				lcolnode = coerce_to_common_type(pstate, lcolnode,
2292												 rescoltype, context);
2293			else if (IsA(lcolnode, Const) ||
2294					 IsA(lcolnode, Param))
2295			{
2296				lcolnode = coerce_to_common_type(pstate, lcolnode,
2297												 rescoltype, context);
2298				ltle->expr = (Expr *) lcolnode;
2299			}
2300
2301			if (rcoltype != UNKNOWNOID)
2302				rcolnode = coerce_to_common_type(pstate, rcolnode,
2303												 rescoltype, context);
2304			else if (IsA(rcolnode, Const) ||
2305					 IsA(rcolnode, Param))
2306			{
2307				rcolnode = coerce_to_common_type(pstate, rcolnode,
2308												 rescoltype, context);
2309				rtle->expr = (Expr *) rcolnode;
2310			}
2311
2312			rescoltypmod = select_common_typmod(pstate,
2313												list_make2(lcolnode, rcolnode),
2314												rescoltype);
2315
2316			/*
2317			 * Select common collation.  A common collation is required for
2318			 * all set operators except UNION ALL; see SQL:2008 7.13 <query
2319			 * expression> Syntax Rule 15c.  (If we fail to identify a common
