2320			 * collation for a UNION ALL column, the colCollations element
2321			 * will be set to InvalidOid, which may result in a runtime error
2322			 * if something at a higher query level wants to use the column's
2323			 * collation.)
2324			 */
2325			rescolcoll = select_common_collation(pstate,
2326												 list_make2(lcolnode, rcolnode),
2327												 (op->op == SETOP_UNION && op->all));
2328
2329			/* emit results */
2330			op->colTypes = lappend_oid(op->colTypes, rescoltype);
2331			op->colTypmods = lappend_int(op->colTypmods, rescoltypmod);
2332			op->colCollations = lappend_oid(op->colCollations, rescolcoll);
2333
2334			/*
2335			 * For all cases except UNION ALL, identify the grouping operators
2336			 * (and, if available, sorting operators) that will be used to
2337			 * eliminate duplicates.
2338			 */
2339			if (op->op != SETOP_UNION || !op->all)
2340			{
2341				ParseCallbackState pcbstate;
2342
2343				setup_parser_errposition_callback(&pcbstate, pstate,
2344												  bestlocation);
2345
2346				/*
2347				 * If it's a recursive union, we need to require hashing
2348				 * support.
2349				 */
2350				op->groupClauses = lappend(op->groupClauses,
2351										   makeSortGroupClauseForSetOp(rescoltype, recursive));
2352
2353				cancel_parser_errposition_callback(&pcbstate);
2354			}
2355
2356			/*
2357			 * Construct a dummy tlist entry to return.  We use a SetToDefault
2358			 * node for the expression, since it carries exactly the fields
2359			 * needed, but any other expression node type would do as well.
2360			 */
2361			if (targetlist)
2362			{
2363				SetToDefault *rescolnode = makeNode(SetToDefault);
2364				TargetEntry *restle;
2365
2366				rescolnode->typeId = rescoltype;
2367				rescolnode->typeMod = rescoltypmod;
2368				rescolnode->collation = rescolcoll;
2369				rescolnode->location = bestlocation;
2370				restle = makeTargetEntry((Expr *) rescolnode,
2371										 0, /* no need to set resno */
2372										 NULL,
2373										 false);
2374				*targetlist = lappend(*targetlist, restle);
2375			}
2376		}
2377
2378		return (Node *) op;
2379	}
2380}
2381
2382/*
2383 * Process the outputs of the non-recursive term of a recursive union
2384 * to set up the parent CTE's columns
2385 */
2386static void
2387determineRecursiveColTypes(ParseState *pstate, Node *larg, List *nrtargetlist)
2388{
2389	Node	   *node;
2390	int			leftmostRTI;
2391	Query	   *leftmostQuery;
2392	List	   *targetList;
2393	ListCell   *left_tlist;
2394	ListCell   *nrtl;
2395	int			next_resno;
2396
2397	/*
2398	 * Find leftmost leaf SELECT
2399	 */
2400	node = larg;
2401	while (node && IsA(node, SetOperationStmt))
2402		node = ((SetOperationStmt *) node)->larg;
2403	Assert(node && IsA(node, RangeTblRef));
2404	leftmostRTI = ((RangeTblRef *) node)->rtindex;
2405	leftmostQuery = rt_fetch(leftmostRTI, pstate->p_rtable)->subquery;
2406	Assert(leftmostQuery != NULL);
2407
2408	/*
2409	 * Generate dummy targetlist using column names of leftmost select and
2410	 * dummy result expressions of the non-recursive term.
2411	 */
2412	targetList = NIL;
2413	next_resno = 1;
2414
2415	forboth(nrtl, nrtargetlist, left_tlist, leftmostQuery->targetList)
2416	{
2417		TargetEntry *nrtle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(nrtl);
2418		TargetEntry *lefttle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(left_tlist);
2419		char	   *colName;
2420		TargetEntry *tle;
2421
2422		Assert(!lefttle->resjunk);
2423		colName = pstrdup(lefttle->resname);
2424		tle = makeTargetEntry(nrtle->expr,
2425							  next_resno++,
2426							  colName,
2427							  false);
2428		targetList = lappend(targetList, tle);
2429	}
2430
2431	/* Now build CTE's output column info using dummy targetlist */
2432	analyzeCTETargetList(pstate, pstate->p_parent_cte, targetList);
2433}
2434
2435
2436/*
2437 * transformReturnStmt -
2438 *	  transforms a return statement
2439 */
2440static Query *
2441transformReturnStmt(ParseState *pstate, ReturnStmt *stmt)
2442{
2443	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
2444
2445	qry->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
2446	qry->isReturn = true;
2447
2448	qry->targetList = list_make1(makeTargetEntry((Expr *) transformExpr(pstate, stmt->returnval, EXPR_KIND_SELECT_TARGET),
2449												 1, NULL, false));
2450
2451	if (pstate->p_resolve_unknowns)
2452		resolveTargetListUnknowns(pstate, qry->targetList);
2453	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
2454	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
2455	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, NULL);
2456	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
2457	qry->hasWindowFuncs = pstate->p_hasWindowFuncs;
2458	qry->hasTargetSRFs = pstate->p_hasTargetSRFs;
2459	qry->hasAggs = pstate->p_hasAggs;
2460
2461	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
2462
2463	return qry;
2464}
2465
2466
2467/*
2468 * transformUpdateStmt -
2469 *	  transforms an update statement
2470 */
2471static Query *
2472transformUpdateStmt(ParseState *pstate, UpdateStmt *stmt)
2473{
2474	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
2475	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
2476	Node	   *qual;
2477
2478	qry->commandType = CMD_UPDATE;
2479	pstate->p_is_insert = false;
2480
2481	/* process the WITH clause independently of all else */
2482	if (stmt->withClause)
2483	{
2484		qry->hasRecursive = stmt->withClause->recursive;
2485		qry->cteList = transformWithClause(pstate, stmt->withClause);
2486		qry->hasModifyingCTE = pstate->p_hasModifyingCTE;
2487	}
2488
2489	qry->resultRelation = setTargetTable(pstate, stmt->relation,
2490										 stmt->relation->inh,
2491										 true,
2492										 ACL_UPDATE);
2493	nsitem = pstate->p_target_nsitem;
2494
2495	/* disallow UPDATE ... WHERE CURRENT OF on a view */
2496	if (stmt->whereClause &&
2497		IsA(stmt->whereClause, CurrentOfExpr) &&
2498		pstate->p_target_relation->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW)
2499		ereport(ERROR,
2500				errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2501				errmsg("WHERE CURRENT OF on a view is not implemented"));
2502
2503	/* subqueries in FROM cannot access the result relation */
2504	nsitem->p_lateral_only = true;
2505	nsitem->p_lateral_ok = false;
2506
2507	/*
2508	 * the FROM clause is non-standard SQL syntax. We used to be able to do
2509	 * this with REPLACE in POSTQUEL so we keep the feature.
2510	 */
2511	transformFromClause(pstate, stmt->fromClause);
2512
2513	/* remaining clauses can reference the result relation normally */
2514	nsitem->p_lateral_only = false;
2515	nsitem->p_lateral_ok = true;
2516
2517	qual = transformWhereClause(pstate, stmt->whereClause,
2518								EXPR_KIND_WHERE, "WHERE");
2519
2520	transformReturningClause(pstate, qry, stmt->returningClause,
2521							 EXPR_KIND_RETURNING);
2522
2523	/*
2524	 * Now we are done with SELECT-like processing, and can get on with
2525	 * transforming the target list to match the UPDATE target columns.
2526	 */
2527	qry->targetList = transformUpdateTargetList(pstate, stmt->targetList);
2528
2529	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
2530	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
2531	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, qual);
2532
2533	qry->hasTargetSRFs = pstate->p_hasTargetSRFs;
2534	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
2535
2536	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
2537
2538	return qry;
2539}
2540
2541/*
2542 * transformUpdateTargetList -
2543 *	handle SET clause in UPDATE/MERGE/INSERT ... ON CONFLICT UPDATE
2544 */
2545List *
2546transformUpdateTargetList(ParseState *pstate, List *origTlist)
2547{
2548	List	   *tlist = NIL;
2549	RTEPermissionInfo *target_perminfo;
2550	ListCell   *orig_tl;
2551	ListCell   *tl;
2552
2553	tlist = transformTargetList(pstate, origTlist,
2554								EXPR_KIND_UPDATE_SOURCE);
2555
2556	/* Prepare to assign non-conflicting resnos to resjunk attributes */
2557	if (pstate->p_next_resno <= RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(pstate->p_target_relation))
2558		pstate->p_next_resno = RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(pstate->p_target_relation) + 1;
2559
2560	/* Prepare non-junk columns for assignment to target table */
2561	target_perminfo = pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_perminfo;
2562	orig_tl = list_head(origTlist);
2563
2564	foreach(tl, tlist)
2565	{
2566		TargetEntry *tle = (TargetEntry *) lfirst(tl);
2567		ResTarget  *origTarget;
2568		int			attrno;
2569
2570		if (tle->resjunk)
2571		{
2572			/*
2573			 * Resjunk nodes need no additional processing, but be sure they
2574			 * have resnos that do not match any target columns; else rewriter
2575			 * or planner might get confused.  They don't need a resname
2576			 * either.
2577			 */
2578			tle->resno = (AttrNumber) pstate->p_next_resno++;
2579			tle->resname = NULL;
2580			continue;
2581		}
2582		if (orig_tl == NULL)
2583			elog(ERROR, "UPDATE target count mismatch --- internal error");
2584		origTarget = lfirst_node(ResTarget, orig_tl);
2585
2586		attrno = attnameAttNum(pstate->p_target_relation,
2587							   origTarget->name, true);
2588		if (attrno == InvalidAttrNumber)
2589			ereport(ERROR,
2590					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
2591					 errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
2592							origTarget->name,
2593							RelationGetRelationName(pstate->p_target_relation)),
2594					 (origTarget->indirection != NIL &&
2595					  strcmp(origTarget->name, pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_names->aliasname) == 0) ?
2596					 errhint("SET target columns cannot be qualified with the relation name.") : 0,
2597					 parser_errposition(pstate, origTarget->location)));
2598
2599		updateTargetListEntry(pstate, tle, origTarget->name,
2600							  attrno,
2601							  origTarget->indirection,
2602							  origTarget->location);
2603
2604		/* Mark the target column as requiring update permissions */
2605		target_perminfo->updatedCols = bms_add_member(target_perminfo->updatedCols,
2606													  attrno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
2607
2608		orig_tl = lnext(origTlist, orig_tl);
2609	}
2610	if (orig_tl != NULL)
2611		elog(ERROR, "UPDATE target count mismatch --- internal error");
2612
2613	return tlist;
2614}
2615
2616/*
2617 * addNSItemForReturning -
2618 *	add a ParseNamespaceItem for the OLD or NEW alias in RETURNING.
2619 */
2620static void
2621addNSItemForReturning(ParseState *pstate, const char *aliasname,
2622					  VarReturningType returning_type)
2623{
2624	List	   *colnames;
2625	int			numattrs;
2626	ParseNamespaceColumn *nscolumns;
2627	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
2628
2629	/* copy per-column data from the target relation */
2630	colnames = pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_rte->eref->colnames;
2631	numattrs = list_length(colnames);
2632
2633	nscolumns = (ParseNamespaceColumn *)
2634		palloc(numattrs * sizeof(ParseNamespaceColumn));
2635
2636	memcpy(nscolumns, pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_nscolumns,
2637		   numattrs * sizeof(ParseNamespaceColumn));
2638
2639	/* mark all columns as returning OLD/NEW */
2640	for (int i = 0; i < numattrs; i++)
2641		nscolumns[i].p_varreturningtype = returning_type;
2642
2643	/* build the nsitem, copying most fields from the target relation */
2644	nsitem = (ParseNamespaceItem *) palloc(sizeof(ParseNamespaceItem));
2645	nsitem->p_names = makeAlias(aliasname, colnames);
2646	nsitem->p_rte = pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_rte;
2647	nsitem->p_rtindex = pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_rtindex;
2648	nsitem->p_perminfo = pstate->p_target_nsitem->p_perminfo;
2649	nsitem->p_nscolumns = nscolumns;
2650	nsitem->p_returning_type = returning_type;
2651
2652	/* add it to the query namespace as a table-only item */
2653	addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, false, true, false);
2654}
2655
2656/*
2657 * transformReturningClause -
2658 *	handle a RETURNING clause in INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/MERGE
2659 */
2660void
2661transformReturningClause(ParseState *pstate, Query *qry,
2662						 ReturningClause *returningClause,
2663						 ParseExprKind exprKind)
2664{
2665	int			save_nslen = list_length(pstate->p_namespace);
2666	int			save_next_resno;
2667
2668	if (returningClause == NULL)
2669		return;					/* nothing to do */
2670
2671	/*
2672	 * Scan RETURNING WITH(...) options for OLD/NEW alias names.  Complain if
2673	 * there is any conflict with existing relations.
2674	 */
2675	foreach_node(ReturningOption, option, returningClause->options)
2676	{
2677		switch (option->option)
2678		{
2679			case RETURNING_OPTION_OLD:
2680				if (qry->returningOldAlias != NULL)
2681					ereport(ERROR,
2682							errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2683					/* translator: %s is OLD or NEW */
2684							errmsg("%s cannot be specified multiple times", "OLD"),
2685							parser_errposition(pstate, option->location));
2686				qry->returningOldAlias = option->value;
2687				break;
2688
2689			case RETURNING_OPTION_NEW:
2690				if (qry->returningNewAlias != NULL)
2691					ereport(ERROR,
2692							errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2693					/* translator: %s is OLD or NEW */
2694							errmsg("%s cannot be specified multiple times", "NEW"),
2695							parser_errposition(pstate, option->location));
2696				qry->returningNewAlias = option->value;
2697				break;
2698
2699			default:
2700				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized returning option: %d", option->option);
2701		}
2702
2703		if (refnameNamespaceItem(pstate, NULL, option->value, -1, NULL) != NULL)
2704			ereport(ERROR,
2705					errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_ALIAS),
2706					errmsg("table name \"%s\" specified more than once",
2707						   option->value),
2708					parser_errposition(pstate, option->location));
2709
2710		addNSItemForReturning(pstate, option->value,
2711							  option->option == RETURNING_OPTION_OLD ?
2712							  VAR_RETURNING_OLD : VAR_RETURNING_NEW);
2713	}
2714
2715	/*
2716	 * If OLD/NEW alias names weren't explicitly specified, use "old"/"new"
2717	 * unless masked by existing relations.
2718	 */
2719	if (qry->returningOldAlias == NULL &&
2720		refnameNamespaceItem(pstate, NULL, "old", -1, NULL) == NULL)
2721	{
2722		qry->returningOldAlias = "old";
2723		addNSItemForReturning(pstate, "old", VAR_RETURNING_OLD);
2724	}
2725	if (qry->returningNewAlias == NULL &&
2726		refnameNamespaceItem(pstate, NULL, "new", -1, NULL) == NULL)
2727	{
2728		qry->returningNewAlias = "new";
2729		addNSItemForReturning(pstate, "new", VAR_RETURNING_NEW);
2730	}
2731
2732	/*
2733	 * We need to assign resnos starting at one in the RETURNING list. Save
2734	 * and restore the main tlist's value of p_next_resno, just in case
2735	 * someone looks at it later (probably won't happen).
2736	 */
2737	save_next_resno = pstate->p_next_resno;
2738	pstate->p_next_resno = 1;
2739
2740	/* transform RETURNING expressions identically to a SELECT targetlist */
2741	qry->returningList = transformTargetList(pstate,
2742											 returningClause->exprs,
2743											 exprKind);
2744
2745	/*
2746	 * Complain if the nonempty tlist expanded to nothing (which is possible
2747	 * if it contains only a star-expansion of a zero-column table).  If we
2748	 * allow this, the parsed Query will look like it didn't have RETURNING,
2749	 * with results that would probably surprise the user.
2750	 */
2751	if (qry->returningList == NIL)
2752		ereport(ERROR,
2753				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2754				 errmsg("RETURNING must have at least one column"),
2755				 parser_errposition(pstate,
2756									exprLocation(linitial(returningClause->exprs)))));
2757
2758	/* mark column origins */
2759	markTargetListOrigins(pstate, qry->returningList);
2760
2761	/* resolve any still-unresolved output columns as being type text */
2762	if (pstate->p_resolve_unknowns)
2763		resolveTargetListUnknowns(pstate, qry->returningList);
2764
2765	/* restore state */
2766	pstate->p_namespace = list_truncate(pstate->p_namespace, save_nslen);
2767	pstate->p_next_resno = save_next_resno;
2768}
2769
2770
2771/*
2772 * transformPLAssignStmt -
2773 *	  transform a PL/pgSQL assignment statement
2774 *
2775 * If there is no opt_indirection, the transformed statement looks like
2776 * "SELECT a_expr ...", except the expression has been cast to the type of
2777 * the target.  With indirection, it's still a SELECT, but the expression will
2778 * incorporate FieldStore and/or assignment SubscriptingRef nodes to compute a
2779 * new value for a container-type variable represented by the target.  The
2780 * expression references the target as the container source.
2781 */
2782static Query *
2783transformPLAssignStmt(ParseState *pstate, PLAssignStmt *stmt)
2784{
2785	Query	   *qry = makeNode(Query);
2786	ColumnRef  *cref = makeNode(ColumnRef);
2787	List	   *indirection = stmt->indirection;
2788	int			nnames = stmt->nnames;
2789	SelectStmt *sstmt = stmt->val;
2790	Node	   *target;
2791	Oid			targettype;
2792	int32		targettypmod;
2793	Oid			targetcollation;
2794	List	   *tlist;
2795	TargetEntry *tle;
2796	Oid			type_id;
2797	Node	   *qual;
2798	ListCell   *l;
2799
2800	/*
2801	 * First, construct a ColumnRef for the target variable.  If the target
2802	 * has more than one dotted name, we have to pull the extra names out of
2803	 * the indirection list.
2804	 */
2805	cref->fields = list_make1(makeString(stmt->name));
2806	cref->location = stmt->location;
2807	if (nnames > 1)
2808	{
2809		/* avoid munging the raw parsetree */
2810		indirection = list_copy(indirection);
2811		while (--nnames > 0 && indirection != NIL)
2812		{
2813			Node	   *ind = (Node *) linitial(indirection);
2814
2815			if (!IsA(ind, String))
2816				elog(ERROR, "invalid name count in PLAssignStmt");
2817			cref->fields = lappend(cref->fields, ind);
2818			indirection = list_delete_first(indirection);
2819		}
2820	}
2821
2822	/*
2823	 * Transform the target reference.  Typically we will get back a Param
2824	 * node, but there's no reason to be too picky about its type.
2825	 */
2826	target = transformExpr(pstate, (Node *) cref,
2827						   EXPR_KIND_UPDATE_TARGET);
2828	targettype = exprType(target);
2829	targettypmod = exprTypmod(target);
2830	targetcollation = exprCollation(target);
2831
2832	/*
2833	 * The rest mostly matches transformSelectStmt, except that we needn't
2834	 * consider WITH or INTO, and we build a targetlist our own way.
2835	 */
2836	qry->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
2837	pstate->p_is_insert = false;
2838
2839	/* make FOR UPDATE/FOR SHARE info available to addRangeTableEntry */
2840	pstate->p_locking_clause = sstmt->lockingClause;
2841
2842	/* make WINDOW info available for window functions, too */
2843	pstate->p_windowdefs = sstmt->windowClause;
2844
2845	/* process the FROM clause */
2846	transformFromClause(pstate, sstmt->fromClause);
2847
2848	/* initially transform the targetlist as if in SELECT */
2849	tlist = transformTargetList(pstate, sstmt->targetList,
2850								EXPR_KIND_SELECT_TARGET);
2851
2852	/* we should have exactly one targetlist item */
2853	if (list_length(tlist) != 1)
2854		ereport(ERROR,
2855				(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2856				 errmsg_plural("assignment source returned %d column",
2857							   "assignment source returned %d columns",
2858							   list_length(tlist),
2859							   list_length(tlist))));
2860
2861	tle = linitial_node(TargetEntry, tlist);
2862
2863	/*
2864	 * This next bit is similar to transformAssignedExpr; the key difference
2865	 * is we use COERCION_PLPGSQL not COERCION_ASSIGNMENT.
2866	 */
2867	type_id = exprType((Node *) tle->expr);
2868
2869	pstate->p_expr_kind = EXPR_KIND_UPDATE_TARGET;
2870
2871	if (indirection)
2872	{
2873		tle->expr = (Expr *)
2874			transformAssignmentIndirection(pstate,
2875										   target,
2876										   stmt->name,
2877										   false,
2878										   targettype,
2879										   targettypmod,
2880										   targetcollation,
2881										   indirection,
2882										   list_head(indirection),
2883										   (Node *) tle->expr,
2884										   COERCION_PLPGSQL,
2885										   exprLocation(target));
2886	}
2887	else if (targettype != type_id &&
2888			 (targettype == RECORDOID || ISCOMPLEX(targettype)) &&
2889			 (type_id == RECORDOID || ISCOMPLEX(type_id)))
2890	{
2891		/*
2892		 * Hack: do not let coerce_to_target_type() deal with inconsistent
2893		 * composite types.  Just pass the expression result through as-is,
2894		 * and let the PL/pgSQL executor do the conversion its way.  This is
2895		 * rather bogus, but it's needed for backwards compatibility.
2896		 */
2897	}
2898	else
2899	{
2900		/*
2901		 * For normal non-qualified target column, do type checking and
2902		 * coercion.
2903		 */
2904		Node	   *orig_expr = (Node *) tle->expr;
2905
2906		tle->expr = (Expr *)
2907			coerce_to_target_type(pstate,
2908								  orig_expr, type_id,
2909								  targettype, targettypmod,
2910								  COERCION_PLPGSQL,
2911								  COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST,
2912								  -1);
2913		/* With COERCION_PLPGSQL, this error is probably unreachable */
2914		if (tle->expr == NULL)
2915			ereport(ERROR,
2916					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
2917					 errmsg("variable \"%s\" is of type %s"
2918							" but expression is of type %s",
2919							stmt->name,
2920							format_type_be(targettype),
2921							format_type_be(type_id)),
2922					 errhint("You will need to rewrite or cast the expression."),
2923					 parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation(orig_expr))));
2924	}
2925
2926	pstate->p_expr_kind = EXPR_KIND_NONE;
2927
2928	qry->targetList = list_make1(tle);
2929
2930	/* transform WHERE */
2931	qual = transformWhereClause(pstate, sstmt->whereClause,
2932								EXPR_KIND_WHERE, "WHERE");
2933
2934	/* initial processing of HAVING clause is much like WHERE clause */
2935	qry->havingQual = transformWhereClause(pstate, sstmt->havingClause,
2936										   EXPR_KIND_HAVING, "HAVING");
2937
2938	/*
2939	 * Transform sorting/grouping stuff.  Do ORDER BY first because both
2940	 * transformGroupClause and transformDistinctClause need the results. Note
2941	 * that these functions can also change the targetList, so it's passed to
2942	 * them by reference.
2943	 */
2944	qry->sortClause = transformSortClause(pstate,
2945										  sstmt->sortClause,
2946										  &qry->targetList,
2947										  EXPR_KIND_ORDER_BY,
2948										  false /* allow SQL92 rules */ );
2949
2950	qry->groupClause = transformGroupClause(pstate,
2951											sstmt->groupClause,
2952											&qry->groupingSets,
2953											&qry->targetList,
2954											qry->sortClause,
2955											EXPR_KIND_GROUP_BY,
2956											false /* allow SQL92 rules */ );
2957	qry->groupDistinct = sstmt->groupDistinct;
2958
2959	if (sstmt->distinctClause == NIL)
2960	{
2961		qry->distinctClause = NIL;
2962		qry->hasDistinctOn = false;
2963	}
2964	else if (linitial(sstmt->distinctClause) == NULL)
2965	{
2966		/* We had SELECT DISTINCT */
2967		qry->distinctClause = transformDistinctClause(pstate,
2968													  &qry->targetList,
2969													  qry->sortClause,
2970													  false);
2971		qry->hasDistinctOn = false;
2972	}
2973	else
2974	{
2975		/* We had SELECT DISTINCT ON */
2976		qry->distinctClause = transformDistinctOnClause(pstate,
2977														sstmt->distinctClause,
2978														&qry->targetList,
2979														qry->sortClause);
2980		qry->hasDistinctOn = true;
2981	}
2982
2983	/* transform LIMIT */
2984	qry->limitOffset = transformLimitClause(pstate, sstmt->limitOffset,
2985											EXPR_KIND_OFFSET, "OFFSET",
2986											sstmt->limitOption);
2987	qry->limitCount = transformLimitClause(pstate, sstmt->limitCount,
2988										   EXPR_KIND_LIMIT, "LIMIT",
2989										   sstmt->limitOption);
2990	qry->limitOption = sstmt->limitOption;
2991
2992	/* transform window clauses after we have seen all window functions */
2993	qry->windowClause = transformWindowDefinitions(pstate,
2994												   pstate->p_windowdefs,
2995												   &qry->targetList);
2996
2997	qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
2998	qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
2999	qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(pstate->p_joinlist, qual);
3000
3001	qry->hasSubLinks = pstate->p_hasSubLinks;
3002	qry->hasWindowFuncs = pstate->p_hasWindowFuncs;
3003	qry->hasTargetSRFs = pstate->p_hasTargetSRFs;
3004	qry->hasAggs = pstate->p_hasAggs;
3005
3006	foreach(l, sstmt->lockingClause)
3007	{
3008		transformLockingClause(pstate, qry,
3009							   (LockingClause *) lfirst(l), false);
3010	}
3011
3012	assign_query_collations(pstate, qry);
3013
3014	/* this must be done after collations, for reliable comparison of exprs */
3015	if (pstate->p_hasAggs || qry->groupClause || qry->groupingSets || qry->havingQual)
3016		parseCheckAggregates(pstate, qry);
3017
3018	return qry;
3019}
3020
3021
3022/*
3023 * transformDeclareCursorStmt -
3024 *	transform a DECLARE CURSOR Statement
3025 *
3026 * DECLARE CURSOR is like other utility statements in that we emit it as a
3027 * CMD_UTILITY Query node; however, we must first transform the contained
3028 * query.  We used to postpone that until execution, but it's really necessary
3029 * to do it during the normal parse analysis phase to ensure that side effects
3030 * of parser hooks happen at the expected time.
3031 */
3032static Query *
3033transformDeclareCursorStmt(ParseState *pstate, DeclareCursorStmt *stmt)
3034{
3035	Query	   *result;
3036	Query	   *query;
3037
3038	if ((stmt->options & CURSOR_OPT_SCROLL) &&
3039		(stmt->options & CURSOR_OPT_NO_SCROLL))
3040		ereport(ERROR,
3041				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_CURSOR_DEFINITION),
3042		/* translator: %s is a SQL keyword */
3043				 errmsg("cannot specify both %s and %s",
3044						"SCROLL", "NO SCROLL")));
3045
3046	if ((stmt->options & CURSOR_OPT_ASENSITIVE) &&
3047		(stmt->options & CURSOR_OPT_INSENSITIVE))
3048		ereport(ERROR,
3049				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_CURSOR_DEFINITION),
3050		/* translator: %s is a SQL keyword */
3051				 errmsg("cannot specify both %s and %s",
3052						"ASENSITIVE", "INSENSITIVE")));
3053
3054	/* Transform contained query, not allowing SELECT INTO */
3055	query = transformStmt(pstate, stmt->query);
3056	stmt->query = (Node *) query;
3057
3058	/* Grammar should not have allowed anything but SELECT */
3059	if (!IsA(query, Query) ||
3060		query->commandType != CMD_SELECT)
3061		elog(ERROR, "unexpected non-SELECT command in DECLARE CURSOR");
3062
3063	/*
3064	 * We also disallow data-modifying WITH in a cursor.  (This could be
3065	 * allowed, but the semantics of when the updates occur might be
3066	 * surprising.)
3067	 */
3068	if (query->hasModifyingCTE)
3069		ereport(ERROR,
3070				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3071				 errmsg("DECLARE CURSOR must not contain data-modifying statements in WITH")));
3072
3073	/* FOR UPDATE and WITH HOLD are not compatible */
3074	if (query->rowMarks != NIL && (stmt->options & CURSOR_OPT_HOLD))
3075		ereport(ERROR,
3076				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3077		/*------
3078		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3079				 errmsg("DECLARE CURSOR WITH HOLD ... %s is not supported",
3080						LCS_asString(((RowMarkClause *)
3081									  linitial(query->rowMarks))->strength)),
3082				 errdetail("Holdable cursors must be READ ONLY.")));
3083
3084	/* FOR UPDATE and SCROLL are not compatible */
3085	if (query->rowMarks != NIL && (stmt->options & CURSOR_OPT_SCROLL))
3086		ereport(ERROR,
3087				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3088		/*------
3089		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3090				 errmsg("DECLARE SCROLL CURSOR ... %s is not supported",
3091						LCS_asString(((RowMarkClause *)
3092									  linitial(query->rowMarks))->strength)),
3093				 errdetail("Scrollable cursors must be READ ONLY.")));
3094
3095	/* FOR UPDATE and INSENSITIVE are not compatible */
3096	if (query->rowMarks != NIL && (stmt->options & CURSOR_OPT_INSENSITIVE))
3097		ereport(ERROR,
3098				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_CURSOR_DEFINITION),
3099		/*------
3100		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3101				 errmsg("DECLARE INSENSITIVE CURSOR ... %s is not valid",
3102						LCS_asString(((RowMarkClause *)
3103									  linitial(query->rowMarks))->strength)),
3104				 errdetail("Insensitive cursors must be READ ONLY.")));
3105
3106	/* represent the command as a utility Query */
3107	result = makeNode(Query);
3108	result->commandType = CMD_UTILITY;
3109	result->utilityStmt = (Node *) stmt;
3110
3111	return result;
3112}
3113
3114
3115/*
3116 * transformExplainStmt -
3117 *	transform an EXPLAIN Statement
3118 *
3119 * EXPLAIN is like other utility statements in that we emit it as a
3120 * CMD_UTILITY Query node; however, we must first transform the contained
3121 * query.  We used to postpone that until execution, but it's really necessary
3122 * to do it during the normal parse analysis phase to ensure that side effects
3123 * of parser hooks happen at the expected time.
3124 */
3125static Query *
3126transformExplainStmt(ParseState *pstate, ExplainStmt *stmt)
3127{
3128	Query	   *result;
3129	bool		generic_plan = false;
3130	Oid		   *paramTypes = NULL;
3131	int			numParams = 0;
3132
3133	/*
3134	 * If we have no external source of parameter definitions, and the
3135	 * GENERIC_PLAN option is specified, then accept variable parameter
3136	 * definitions (similarly to PREPARE, for example).
3137	 */
3138	if (pstate->p_paramref_hook == NULL)
3139	{
3140		ListCell   *lc;
3141
3142		foreach(lc, stmt->options)
3143		{
3144			DefElem    *opt = (DefElem *) lfirst(lc);
3145
3146			if (strcmp(opt->defname, "generic_plan") == 0)
3147				generic_plan = defGetBoolean(opt);
3148			/* don't "break", as we want the last value */
3149		}
3150		if (generic_plan)
3151			setup_parse_variable_parameters(pstate, &paramTypes, &numParams);
3152	}
3153
3154	/* transform contained query, allowing SELECT INTO */
3155	stmt->query = (Node *) transformOptionalSelectInto(pstate, stmt->query);
3156
3157	/* make sure all is well with parameter types */
3158	if (generic_plan)
3159		check_variable_parameters(pstate, (Query *) stmt->query);
3160
3161	/* represent the command as a utility Query */
3162	result = makeNode(Query);
3163	result->commandType = CMD_UTILITY;
3164	result->utilityStmt = (Node *) stmt;
3165
3166	return result;
3167}
3168
3169
3170/*
3171 * transformCreateTableAsStmt -
3172 *	transform a CREATE TABLE AS, SELECT ... INTO, or CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW
3173 *	Statement
3174 *
3175 * As with DECLARE CURSOR and EXPLAIN, transform the contained statement now.
3176 */
3177static Query *
3178transformCreateTableAsStmt(ParseState *pstate, CreateTableAsStmt *stmt)
3179{
3180	Query	   *result;
3181	Query	   *query;
3182
3183	/* transform contained query, not allowing SELECT INTO */
3184	query = transformStmt(pstate, stmt->query);
3185	stmt->query = (Node *) query;
3186
3187	/* additional work needed for CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW */
3188	if (stmt->objtype == OBJECT_MATVIEW)
3189	{
3190		/*
3191		 * Prohibit a data-modifying CTE in the query used to create a
3192		 * materialized view. It's not sufficiently clear what the user would
3193		 * want to happen if the MV is refreshed or incrementally maintained.
3194		 */
3195		if (query->hasModifyingCTE)
3196			ereport(ERROR,
3197					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3198					 errmsg("materialized views must not use data-modifying statements in WITH")));
3199
3200		/*
3201		 * Check whether any temporary database objects are used in the
3202		 * creation query. It would be hard to refresh data or incrementally
3203		 * maintain it if a source disappeared.
3204		 */
3205		if (isQueryUsingTempRelation(query))
3206			ereport(ERROR,
3207					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3208					 errmsg("materialized views must not use temporary tables or views")));
3209
3210		/*
3211		 * A materialized view would either need to save parameters for use in
3212		 * maintaining/loading the data or prohibit them entirely.  The latter
3213		 * seems safer and more sane.
3214		 */
3215		if (query_contains_extern_params(query))
3216			ereport(ERROR,
3217					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3218					 errmsg("materialized views may not be defined using bound parameters")));
3219
3220		/*
3221		 * For now, we disallow unlogged materialized views, because it seems
3222		 * like a bad idea for them to just go to empty after a crash. (If we
3223		 * could mark them as unpopulated, that would be better, but that
3224		 * requires catalog changes which crash recovery can't presently
3225		 * handle.)
3226		 */
3227		if (stmt->into->rel->relpersistence == RELPERSISTENCE_UNLOGGED)
3228			ereport(ERROR,
3229					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3230					 errmsg("materialized views cannot be unlogged")));
3231
3232		/*
3233		 * At runtime, we'll need a copy of the parsed-but-not-rewritten Query
3234		 * for purposes of creating the view's ON SELECT rule.  We stash that
3235		 * in the IntoClause because that's where intorel_startup() can
3236		 * conveniently get it from.
3237		 */
3238		stmt->into->viewQuery = copyObject(query);
3239	}
3240
3241	/* represent the command as a utility Query */
3242	result = makeNode(Query);
3243	result->commandType = CMD_UTILITY;
3244	result->utilityStmt = (Node *) stmt;
3245
3246	return result;
3247}
3248
3249/*
3250 * transform a CallStmt
3251 */
3252static Query *
3253transformCallStmt(ParseState *pstate, CallStmt *stmt)
3254{
3255	List	   *targs;
3256	ListCell   *lc;
3257	Node	   *node;
3258	FuncExpr   *fexpr;
3259	HeapTuple	proctup;
3260	Datum		proargmodes;
3261	bool		isNull;
3262	List	   *outargs = NIL;
3263	Query	   *result;
3264
3265	/*
3266	 * First, do standard parse analysis on the procedure call and its
3267	 * arguments, allowing us to identify the called procedure.
3268	 */
3269	targs = NIL;
3270	foreach(lc, stmt->funccall->args)
3271	{
3272		targs = lappend(targs, transformExpr(pstate,
3273											 (Node *) lfirst(lc),
3274											 EXPR_KIND_CALL_ARGUMENT));
3275	}
3276
3277	node = ParseFuncOrColumn(pstate,
3278							 stmt->funccall->funcname,
3279							 targs,
3280							 pstate->p_last_srf,
3281							 stmt->funccall,
3282							 true,
3283							 stmt->funccall->location);
3284
3285	assign_expr_collations(pstate, node);
3286
3287	fexpr = castNode(FuncExpr, node);
3288
3289	proctup = SearchSysCache1(PROCOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(fexpr->funcid));
3290	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(proctup))
3291		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for function %u", fexpr->funcid);
3292
3293	/*
3294	 * Expand the argument list to deal with named-argument notation and
3295	 * default arguments.  For ordinary FuncExprs this'd be done during
3296	 * planning, but a CallStmt doesn't go through planning, and there seems
3297	 * no good reason not to do it here.
3298	 */
3299	fexpr->args = expand_function_arguments(fexpr->args,
3300											true,
3301											fexpr->funcresulttype,
3302											proctup);
3303
3304	/* Fetch proargmodes; if it's null, there are no output args */
3305	proargmodes = SysCacheGetAttr(PROCOID, proctup,
3306								  Anum_pg_proc_proargmodes,
3307								  &isNull);
3308	if (!isNull)
3309	{
3310		/*
3311		 * Split the list into input arguments in fexpr->args and output
3312		 * arguments in stmt->outargs.  INOUT arguments appear in both lists.
3313		 */
3314		ArrayType  *arr;
3315		int			numargs;
3316		char	   *argmodes;
3317		List	   *inargs;
3318		int			i;
3319
3320		arr = DatumGetArrayTypeP(proargmodes);	/* ensure not toasted */
3321		numargs = list_length(fexpr->args);
3322		if (ARR_NDIM(arr) != 1 ||
3323			ARR_DIMS(arr)[0] != numargs ||
3324			ARR_HASNULL(arr) ||
3325			ARR_ELEMTYPE(arr) != CHAROID)
3326			elog(ERROR, "proargmodes is not a 1-D char array of length %d or it contains nulls",
3327				 numargs);
3328		argmodes = (char *) ARR_DATA_PTR(arr);
3329
3330		inargs = NIL;
3331		i = 0;
3332		foreach(lc, fexpr->args)
3333		{
3334			Node	   *n = lfirst(lc);
3335
3336			switch (argmodes[i])
3337			{
3338				case PROARGMODE_IN:
3339				case PROARGMODE_VARIADIC:
3340					inargs = lappend(inargs, n);
3341					break;
3342				case PROARGMODE_OUT:
3343					outargs = lappend(outargs, n);
3344					break;
3345				case PROARGMODE_INOUT:
3346					inargs = lappend(inargs, n);
3347					outargs = lappend(outargs, copyObject(n));
3348					break;
3349				default:
3350					/* note we don't support PROARGMODE_TABLE */
3351					elog(ERROR, "invalid argmode %c for procedure",
3352						 argmodes[i]);
3353					break;
3354			}
3355			i++;
3356		}
3357		fexpr->args = inargs;
3358	}
3359
3360	stmt->funcexpr = fexpr;
3361	stmt->outargs = outargs;
3362
3363	ReleaseSysCache(proctup);
3364
3365	/* represent the command as a utility Query */
3366	result = makeNode(Query);
3367	result->commandType = CMD_UTILITY;
3368	result->utilityStmt = (Node *) stmt;
3369
3370	return result;
3371}
3372
3373/*
3374 * Produce a string representation of a LockClauseStrength value.
3375 * This should only be applied to valid values (not LCS_NONE).
3376 */
3377const char *
3378LCS_asString(LockClauseStrength strength)
3379{
3380	switch (strength)
3381	{
3382		case LCS_NONE:
3383			Assert(false);
3384			break;
3385		case LCS_FORKEYSHARE:
3386			return "FOR KEY SHARE";
3387		case LCS_FORSHARE:
3388			return "FOR SHARE";
3389		case LCS_FORNOKEYUPDATE:
3390			return "FOR NO KEY UPDATE";
3391		case LCS_FORUPDATE:
3392			return "FOR UPDATE";
3393	}
3394	return "FOR some";			/* shouldn't happen */
3395}
3396
3397/*
3398 * Check for features that are not supported with FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE.
3399 *
3400 * exported so planner can check again after rewriting, query pullup, etc
3401 */
3402void
3403CheckSelectLocking(Query *qry, LockClauseStrength strength)
3404{
3405	Assert(strength != LCS_NONE);	/* else caller error */
3406
3407	if (qry->setOperations)
3408		ereport(ERROR,
3409				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3410		/*------
3411		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3412				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT",
3413						LCS_asString(strength))));
3414	if (qry->distinctClause != NIL)
3415		ereport(ERROR,
3416				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3417		/*------
3418		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3419				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with DISTINCT clause",
3420						LCS_asString(strength))));
3421	if (qry->groupClause != NIL || qry->groupingSets != NIL)
3422		ereport(ERROR,
3423				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3424		/*------
3425		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3426				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with GROUP BY clause",
3427						LCS_asString(strength))));
3428	if (qry->havingQual != NULL)
3429		ereport(ERROR,
3430				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3431		/*------
3432		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3433				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with HAVING clause",
3434						LCS_asString(strength))));
3435	if (qry->hasAggs)
3436		ereport(ERROR,
3437				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3438		/*------
3439		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3440				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with aggregate functions",
3441						LCS_asString(strength))));
3442	if (qry->hasWindowFuncs)
3443		ereport(ERROR,
3444				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3445		/*------
3446		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3447				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with window functions",
3448						LCS_asString(strength))));
3449	if (qry->hasTargetSRFs)
3450		ereport(ERROR,
3451				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3452		/*------
3453		  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3454				 errmsg("%s is not allowed with set-returning functions in the target list",
3455						LCS_asString(strength))));
3456}
3457
3458/*
3459 * Transform a FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE clause
3460 *
3461 * This basically involves replacing names by integer relids.
3462 *
3463 * NB: if you need to change this, see also markQueryForLocking()
3464 * in rewriteHandler.c, and isLockedRefname() in parse_relation.c.
3465 */
3466static void
3467transformLockingClause(ParseState *pstate, Query *qry, LockingClause *lc,
3468					   bool pushedDown)
3469{
3470	List	   *lockedRels = lc->lockedRels;
3471	ListCell   *l;
3472	ListCell   *rt;
3473	Index		i;
3474	LockingClause *allrels;
3475
3476	CheckSelectLocking(qry, lc->strength);
3477
3478	/* make a clause we can pass down to subqueries to select all rels */
3479	allrels = makeNode(LockingClause);
3480	allrels->lockedRels = NIL;	/* indicates all rels */
3481	allrels->strength = lc->strength;
3482	allrels->waitPolicy = lc->waitPolicy;
3483
3484	if (lockedRels == NIL)
3485	{
3486		/*
3487		 * Lock all regular tables used in query and its subqueries.  We
3488		 * examine inFromCl to exclude auto-added RTEs, particularly NEW/OLD
3489		 * in rules.  This is a bit of an abuse of a mostly-obsolete flag, but
3490		 * it's convenient.  We can't rely on the namespace mechanism that has
3491		 * largely replaced inFromCl, since for example we need to lock
3492		 * base-relation RTEs even if they are masked by upper joins.
3493		 */
3494		i = 0;
3495		foreach(rt, qry->rtable)
3496		{
3497			RangeTblEntry *rte = (RangeTblEntry *) lfirst(rt);
3498
3499			++i;
3500			if (!rte->inFromCl)
3501				continue;
3502			switch (rte->rtekind)
3503			{
3504				case RTE_RELATION:
3505					{
3506						RTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;
3507
3508						applyLockingClause(qry, i,
3509										   lc->strength,
3510										   lc->waitPolicy,
3511										   pushedDown);
3512						perminfo = getRTEPermissionInfo(qry->rteperminfos, rte);
3513						perminfo->requiredPerms |= ACL_SELECT_FOR_UPDATE;
3514					}
3515					break;
3516				case RTE_SUBQUERY:
3517					applyLockingClause(qry, i, lc->strength, lc->waitPolicy,
3518									   pushedDown);
3519
3520					/*
3521					 * FOR UPDATE/SHARE of subquery is propagated to all of
3522					 * subquery's rels, too.  We could do this later (based on
3523					 * the marking of the subquery RTE) but it is convenient
3524					 * to have local knowledge in each query level about which
3525					 * rels need to be opened with RowShareLock.
3526					 */
3527					transformLockingClause(pstate, rte->subquery,
3528										   allrels, true);
3529					break;
3530				default:
3531					/* ignore JOIN, SPECIAL, FUNCTION, VALUES, CTE RTEs */
3532					break;
3533			}
3534		}
3535	}
3536	else
3537	{
3538		/*
3539		 * Lock just the named tables.  As above, we allow locking any base
3540		 * relation regardless of alias-visibility rules, so we need to
3541		 * examine inFromCl to exclude OLD/NEW.
3542		 */
3543		foreach(l, lockedRels)
3544		{
3545			RangeVar   *thisrel = (RangeVar *) lfirst(l);
3546
3547			/* For simplicity we insist on unqualified alias names here */
3548			if (thisrel->catalogname || thisrel->schemaname)
3549				ereport(ERROR,
3550						(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3551				/*------
3552				  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3553						 errmsg("%s must specify unqualified relation names",
3554								LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3555						 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3556
3557			i = 0;
3558			foreach(rt, qry->rtable)
3559			{
3560				RangeTblEntry *rte = (RangeTblEntry *) lfirst(rt);
3561				char	   *rtename = rte->eref->aliasname;
3562
3563				++i;
3564				if (!rte->inFromCl)
3565					continue;
3566
3567				/*
3568				 * A join RTE without an alias is not visible as a relation
3569				 * name and needs to be skipped (otherwise it might hide a
3570				 * base relation with the same name), except if it has a USING
3571				 * alias, which *is* visible.
3572				 *
3573				 * Subquery and values RTEs without aliases are never visible
3574				 * as relation names and must always be skipped.
3575				 */
3576				if (rte->alias == NULL)
3577				{
3578					if (rte->rtekind == RTE_JOIN)
3579					{
3580						if (rte->join_using_alias == NULL)
3581							continue;
3582						rtename = rte->join_using_alias->aliasname;
3583					}
3584					else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY ||
3585							 rte->rtekind == RTE_VALUES)
3586						continue;
3587				}
3588
3589				if (strcmp(rtename, thisrel->relname) == 0)
3590				{
3591					switch (rte->rtekind)
3592					{
3593						case RTE_RELATION:
3594							{
3595								RTEPermissionInfo *perminfo;
3596
3597								applyLockingClause(qry, i,
3598												   lc->strength,
3599												   lc->waitPolicy,
3600												   pushedDown);
3601								perminfo = getRTEPermissionInfo(qry->rteperminfos, rte);
3602								perminfo->requiredPerms |= ACL_SELECT_FOR_UPDATE;
3603							}
3604							break;
3605						case RTE_SUBQUERY:
3606							applyLockingClause(qry, i, lc->strength,
3607											   lc->waitPolicy, pushedDown);
3608							/* see comment above */
3609							transformLockingClause(pstate, rte->subquery,
3610												   allrels, true);
3611							break;
3612						case RTE_JOIN:
3613							ereport(ERROR,
3614									(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3615							/*------
3616							  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3617									 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to a join",
3618											LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3619									 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3620							break;
3621						case RTE_FUNCTION:
3622							ereport(ERROR,
3623									(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3624							/*------
3625							  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3626									 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to a function",
3627											LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3628									 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3629							break;
3630						case RTE_TABLEFUNC:
3631							ereport(ERROR,
3632									(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3633							/*------
3634							  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3635									 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to a table function",
3636											LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3637									 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3638							break;
3639						case RTE_VALUES:
3640							ereport(ERROR,
3641									(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3642							/*------
3643							  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3644									 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to VALUES",
3645											LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3646									 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3647							break;
3648						case RTE_CTE:
3649							ereport(ERROR,
3650									(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3651							/*------
3652							  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3653									 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to a WITH query",
3654											LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3655									 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3656							break;
3657						case RTE_NAMEDTUPLESTORE:
3658							ereport(ERROR,
3659									(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3660							/*------
3661							  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3662									 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to a named tuplestore",
3663											LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3664									 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3665							break;
3666
3667							/* Shouldn't be possible to see RTE_RESULT here */
3668
3669						default:
3670							elog(ERROR, "unrecognized RTE type: %d",
3671								 (int) rte->rtekind);
3672							break;
3673					}
3674					break;		/* out of foreach loop */
3675				}
3676			}
3677			if (rt == NULL)
3678				ereport(ERROR,
3679						(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_TABLE),
3680				/*------
3681				  translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
3682						 errmsg("relation \"%s\" in %s clause not found in FROM clause",
3683								thisrel->relname,
3684								LCS_asString(lc->strength)),
3685						 parser_errposition(pstate, thisrel->location)));
3686		}
3687	}
3688}
3689
3690/*
3691 * Record locking info for a single rangetable item
3692 */
3693void
3694applyLockingClause(Query *qry, Index rtindex,
3695				   LockClauseStrength strength, LockWaitPolicy waitPolicy,
3696				   bool pushedDown)
3697{
3698	RowMarkClause *rc;
3699
3700	Assert(strength != LCS_NONE);	/* else caller error */
3701
3702	/* If it's an explicit clause, make sure hasForUpdate gets set */
3703	if (!pushedDown)
3704		qry->hasForUpdate = true;
3705
3706	/* Check for pre-existing entry for same rtindex */
3707	if ((rc = get_parse_rowmark(qry, rtindex)) != NULL)
3708	{
3709		/*
3710		 * If the same RTE is specified with more than one locking strength,
3711		 * use the strongest.  (Reasonable, since you can't take both a shared
3712		 * and exclusive lock at the same time; it'll end up being exclusive
3713		 * anyway.)
3714		 *
3715		 * Similarly, if the same RTE is specified with more than one lock
3716		 * wait policy, consider that NOWAIT wins over SKIP LOCKED, which in
3717		 * turn wins over waiting for the lock (the default).  This is a bit
3718		 * more debatable but raising an error doesn't seem helpful. (Consider
3719		 * for instance SELECT FOR UPDATE NOWAIT from a view that internally
3720		 * contains a plain FOR UPDATE spec.)  Having NOWAIT win over SKIP
3721		 * LOCKED is reasonable since the former throws an error in case of
3722		 * coming across a locked tuple, which may be undesirable in some
3723		 * cases but it seems better than silently returning inconsistent
3724		 * results.
3725		 *
3726		 * And of course pushedDown becomes false if any clause is explicit.
3727		 */
3728		rc->strength = Max(rc->strength, strength);
3729		rc->waitPolicy = Max(rc->waitPolicy, waitPolicy);
3730		rc->pushedDown &= pushedDown;
3731		return;
3732	}
3733
3734	/* Make a new RowMarkClause */
3735	rc = makeNode(RowMarkClause);
3736	rc->rti = rtindex;
3737	rc->strength = strength;
3738	rc->waitPolicy = waitPolicy;
3739	rc->pushedDown = pushedDown;
3740	qry->rowMarks = lappend(qry->rowMarks, rc);
3741}
3742
3743#ifdef DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED
3744/*
3745 * Coverage testing for raw_expression_tree_walker().
3746 *
3747 * When enabled, we run raw_expression_tree_walker() over every DML statement
3748 * submitted to parse analysis.  Without this provision, that function is only
3749 * applied in limited cases involving CTEs, and we don't really want to have
3750 * to test everything inside as well as outside a CTE.
3751 */
3752static bool
3753test_raw_expression_coverage(Node *node, void *context)
3754{
3755	if (node == NULL)
3756		return false;
3757	return raw_expression_tree_walker(node,
3758									  test_raw_expression_coverage,
3759									  context);
3760}
3761#endif							/* DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED */
